Ayahs

الٓر ۚ كِتَـٰبٌ أُحْكِمَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥ ثُمَّ فُصِّلَتْ مِن لَّدُنْ حَكِيمٍ خَبِيرٍ (۱)
Alif, Lam Ra. [This is] a Book, whose signs have been made definitive and then elaborated, from One [who is] all-wise, all-aware, (1)
أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّنِى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ (۲)
declaring: ‘Worship no one but Allah. I am indeed a warner to you from Him and a bearer of good news. (2)

Key Points

The Quran is composed of these very letters of the alphabet, which are always available to everyone. However, Allah has fashioned a Book from these simple letters that no one can produce its like. Just as man makes bricks and pottery from clay, Allah creates thousands of types of flowers, plants, fruits, animals, and humans from the same clay. Yes, just as we observe the power of Allah in the system of creation (Taqwin), we also observe His power in the Book of legislation (Tashri'). The Quran is a Book that is both firm (muhkam) and detailed (mufassal); it contains neither contradiction nor inconsistency; it is both eloquent (fasih) and expressive (baligh). Its content, across various chapters and surahs, appears separately yet consecutively and continuously. The Quran is a firm Book that possesses a single spirit, and that spirit is the spirit of Monotheism (Tawhid). All the stories, admonitions, rulings, and verses of the Quran are the detailed elaboration of that spirit of Tawhid. The Quran is a firm Book; it has neither been altered nor corrupted, nor is it subject to doubt or skepticism. Its foundation is not based on conjecture or assumption, but all its contents are firm and solid.

Messages

  1. The Quran is a very important Book. (The indefinite form and tanwin of kitabun [كتابٌ] signify its greatness.)
  2. The words and content of the Quran are signs of its greatness. (Ayah)
  3. The foundation of the Quran is firm and solid, and nothing can cause it to waver. Rather, with the advancement of science, the secrets of this Book are further revealed. (Uhkimat Ayatuh)
  4. A law and a book must be both firm and clear and explicit. (Uhkimat... Thumma Fussilat) (Non-divine laws are either not firm, or not clear, or the legislator has not enacted the law based on wisdom, expediency, and knowledge.)
  5. The Quran is both argumentative and unshakable, and comprehensive of all dimensions of worldly and otherworldly life. (Uhkimat... Thumma Fussilat)
  6. The general principles of the Quran were revealed all at once upon the heart of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and then detailed over a period of twenty-three years. (Uhkimat... Thumma Fussilat)
  7. Because Allah is Wise (Hakim), His Book is firm (muhkam), and because He is All-Aware (Khabir), He has detailed all needs. (Uhkimat... Thumma Fussilat min ladun Hakim Khabir)
  8. The call to monotheism is the headline message of all the Prophets. (Illā ta‘budū illā Allāh)
  9. The philosophy behind the revelation of heavenly Books is Monotheism (Tawhid) and the purification of society from polytheism (Shirk). (Kitābun uhkimat āyātuh... Illā ta‘budū illā Allāh)
  10. The call to monotheism, warning, and glad tidings are examples of Allah's firm speech. (Uhkimat āyātuh... Illā ta‘budū illā Allāh)
  11. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله came from Allah, but for the happiness of the people. (Innanī lakum minhu)
  12. The method of propagation used by the Prophets is warning and giving glad tidings. (Nadhīrun wa Bashīr)

Explanations

This Surah was revealed in the final years of the Prophet's صلى الله عليه وآله presence in Mecca, years during which the Muslims were living under extremely difficult conditions due to the passing of Hadrat Abu Talib and Hadrat Khadijah. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Surah Hud made me old (5). This is because the verse of steadfastness was revealed in this Surah: «فاستقم كما امرت و من تاب معك» (6) (Be steadfast as you have been commanded, and those who repent with you). Be patient and steadfast just as you have been commanded, and your companions must also be steadfast. However, the impatience of the Prophet's صلى الله عليه وآله companions distressed him. The content of this Surah, which has 123 verses, is the history of the Prophets, especially Hadrat Nuh عليه السلام, and attention to matters of belief. By carefully studying the history of the Prophets, one concludes that the movement of the Prophets is a continuous historical process, not a fleeting incident. The history of the Prophets and their nations is governed by laws, and studying it clarifies the secret behind the downfall or honor of nations. It shows that religion is not separate from society, and the destiny of a society is entirely dependent on the religion and creed of its people. The name of Hadrat Hud عليه السلام is mentioned five times in this Surah, which is equal to the repetition of this name in the rest of the Quran.

(5) Tafsir Namuneh; Bihar, Vol. 92, p. 198. (6) Hud, 112.

Ayah

وَأَنِ ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ يُمَتِّعْكُم مَّتَـٰعًا حَسَنًا إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى وَيُؤْتِ كُلَّ ذِى فَضْلٍ فَضْلَهُۥ ۖ وَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ كَبِيرٍ (۳)
Plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. He will provide you with a good provision for a specified term and grant His grace to every meritorious person. But if you turn your backs [on Him], indeed I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day. (3)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Tawhid (Monotheism) was discussed, and in this Ayah, the return to Allah and remaining within the orbit of Tawhid is addressed.

«مَتاعاً حَسناً» (a good enjoyment) means a pleasant life accompanied by spiritual tranquility, in contrast to those who, due to turning away from the remembrance of Allah, have a ma'ishah ḍank (a straitened life) and a bitter existence, even if they are materially comfortable and prosperous.

Messages

1- One of the duties of the Prophets is the reconstruction (rehabilitation) of those who have deviated. «استغفروا ربّكم ثمّ توبوا»

2- Unless a person purifies himself from sin and becomes lighthearted (unburdened), he cannot soar towards the Truth. «استغفروا ربّكم ثمّ توبوا»

3- Erasing sin is not enough; movement towards Allah is necessary. «استغفروا ربّكم ثمّ توبوا»

4- Repentance (Tawbah) is obligatory. «توبوا الیه»

5- Repentance from sin also causes the return of Allah's material favors. «توبوا... یمتّعكم»

6- The resources available to disbelievers and sinners are not blessed or good because they are used for corruption (fasad) and mischief (fitnah). «متاعاً حسناً»

7- A good life is a focus of Islam. «متاعاً حسناً»

8- Faith and repentance insure a person's life until the end of his term. «یمتّعكم... الى اجلٍ مسمّى »

9- Repentance is the key to qualitative and quantitative success. «متاعاً حسناً الى اجلٍ مسمّى »

10- Repentance is a factor that prevents sudden deaths. «الى اجلٍ مسمّى »

11- The date and time of death are unknown to man. «الى اجلٍ مسمّى »

12- Ranks in the Hereafter are different. Whoever acquires more merit (faḍl) in this world will receive greater favor in the Hereafter. «یؤت كلّ ذى فضلٍ فضله»

13- The Prophets are compassionate towards the people. The phrase «انى اخاف علیكم» (Indeed, I fear for you) appears frequently in the Quran.

Ayahs

إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٤)
To Allah will be your return, and He has power over all things.’ (4)
أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ يَثْنُونَ صُدُورَهُمْ لِيَسْتَخْفُوا۟ مِنْهُ ۚ أَلَا حِينَ يَسْتَغْشُونَ ثِيَابَهُمْ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (٥)
Look! They fold up their breasts to hide [their secret feelings] from Him. Look! When they draw their cloaks over their heads, He knows whatever they keep secret and whatever they disclose. Indeed, He knows best whatever is in the breasts. (5)

Key Points

«یَثنُون» means bringing two things close together and bending them (اثنان means two), and it is also used to mean harboring malice in the heart. Therefore, the phrase «یثنون صدورهم» either means that the disbelievers bring their chests close together for confidential talks and plotting against the prophets, or it means that they harbor malice against the prophets in their hearts. In Ayah 7 of Surah Nuh, we read: «جعلوا اصابعهم فى اذانهم و استغشوا ثیابهم», meaning the disbelievers put their fingers in their ears and cover themselves with their garments so as not to hear the call of the prophets. The two most important objections of those who deny the Resurrection are: A: What power can revive the dead? Ayah 4 answers this: «هو على كل شیىٍ قدیر» (Allah is capable of everything). B: How will the scattered particles be gathered in one place? Ayah 5 answers this: «انه علیم بذات الصدور» (He knows what is in the hearts/chests).

Messages

  1. Our return is only to Allah, not to others. «الى اللّه مرجعكم»

  2. In the previous verses, Monotheism (Tawhid) and Prophethood were discussed; this verse addresses the principle of Resurrection (Ma'ad). «الى اللّه مرجعكم»

  3. Assuming you hide your sinister goals from the Prophet, Allah is the One Who knows the secrets of the hearts. «یَثنون صدورهم لیستخفوا منه... انّه علیم بذات الصدور»

Ayah

۞ وَمَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ رِزْقُهَا وَيَعْلَمُ مُسْتَقَرَّهَا وَمُسْتَوْدَعَهَا ۚ كُلٌّ فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (٦)
There is no animal on the earth, but that its sustenance lies with Allah, and He knows its [enduring] abode and its temporary place of lodging. Everything is in a manifest Book. (6)

Key Points

The word dābbah (دابّة) derives from dabīb (دَبیب), meaning to walk slowly and take small steps, but it is applied to every moving creature. Rizq (رزق) means continuous provision, whether material or spiritual. Therefore, in supplication, we read: "O Allah, grant me... a humble heart... a remembering tongue... and beneficial knowledge."(7)

The provision that Allah grants is suitable for creatures in every respect. For example, the food of a child in the mother's womb changes according to its needs until, after birth, it turns into palatable milk. Otherwise, by what power could a human deliver food to an infant in the mother's womb, or how could the infant obtain its own food?

"و كایّن من دابةٍ لاتحمل رزقها الله یرزقها"(8)

Mustaqarr (مستقرّ) means a fixed abode, and mustawda' (مستودع) means depositing something and taking it back, which is a metaphor for a temporary abode.

Imam Ali عليه السلام, in his testament to his son Imam Hassan, says: Provision (rizq) is of two types: one type you must acquire through effort and striving, and another type comes to you on its own.(9) (Such as light, rain, and air in nature, and intelligence, memory, and talent in humans.)

Therefore, the intent of the Ayah is not that we should sit in a corner and wait for heavenly food to descend. Rather, the purpose is that we should both work and strive, and also recognize whatever we acquire as provision from Allah. Hence, all our religious leaders used to work.

The prerequisite for providing provision to all creatures is knowledge of their location and needs. There must be an extremely precise system that knows the latest statistics, the latest needs, and the latest dwelling places. He must know the number of humans and animals—marine, aerial, and terrestrial—and all creatures, large and small, with their various characteristics and highly diverse needs. "یعلم مستقرها و مستودعها"

If all matters are in a Clear Book (Kitāb Mubīn)—"كلٌ فى كتاب مبین" (everything is in a Clear Book)—then whoever possesses the knowledge of the Book knows everything. In the last Ayah of Sūrat Ar-Ra‘d, we read: Say to the disbelievers: My witness is Allah and whoever possesses the knowledge of the Book. Shi‘a and Sunni narrations state that the person referred to is Imam Ali عليه السلام.

Allah has two traditions (Sunan): one is the tradition of providing provision, and the other is the tradition of executing affairs through means and causes. Therefore, attaining provision does not contradict the law of human effort.

7) Bihar, Vol. 95, p. 258. 8) Al-‘Ankabūt, 60. 9) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 147.

Messages

1- Every living creature has the right to sustenance, and Allah has made this right incumbent upon Himself. "ما من دابّة فى الارض الاّ على اللّه رزقها"

2- Our provision is in the hands of Allah; we should not bow our necks before others. "ما من دابّة... الاّ على اللّه رزقها"

3- Regarding sustenance, we should neither be greedy nor fearful. "و ما من دابّة ... الاّ على اللّه رزقها"

4- Allah has placed the sources of provision at our disposal and has also granted us the intellect and means for its extraction, discovery, and acquisition. "الاّ على اللّه رزقها"

5- Wherever we go, we are under the supervision of Allah, whether traveling or settled. "یعلم مستقرّها و مستودعها"

6- All information and statistics of this universe are precisely recorded and registered in a central place. "كلٌ فى كتاب مبین"

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ وَكَانَ عَرْشُهُۥ عَلَى ٱلْمَآءِ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا ۗ وَلَئِن قُلْتَ إِنَّكُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ٱلْمَوْتِ لَيَقُولَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷)
It is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days—and His Throne was [then] upon the waters—that He may test you [to see] which of you is best in conduct. Yet if you say, ‘You will indeed be raised up after death,’ the faithless will surely say, ‘This is nothing but plain magic.’ (7)

Key Points

The meaning of "six days" in the Ayah is six stages and periods. This is because, at the beginning of creation, there was no sun for there to be night and day. The word "عَرش" (Arsh) is applied to a roof, a covered place, scaffolding, and also to the high and great throne of kings. Sometimes it is used metaphorically to denote power. For example, they say: So-and-so ascended the throne, or so-and-so was pulled down from the throne, meaning his power was destroyed.

Question: This Ayah considers the goal of creation to be the testing of man, «لیَبلوَكم» (that He might test you), but other Ayahs state other matters. How is this reconciled?

Answer: The goals are sequential and staged, just as plowing the earth is for planting, and planting is for wheat, and wheat is for bread, and bread is for man. The Ayahs of the Quran have also stated stages for the goal of human creation:

A: Creation is for testing. «لِیَبلوَكم» B: Testing is for separating the good from the wicked. «لِیمیز اللّه الخبیث من الطیّب» (10) C: Separating the good from the wicked is for appropriate reward and punishment. «لتُجزى كل نفس بما كسبت» (11) D: Appropriate reward and punishment is for fulfilling the promise. «وعداً علینا» (12)

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Allah’s testing is not for the sake of knowing, but His testing is for the manifestation of man's reaction, so that if his deed is good, he receives a reward, and if it is bad, he receives punishment. (13)

In narrations, «احسن عملا» (best in deed) has been interpreted as «احسن عقلا و اورع عن محارم اللّه و اسرع فى طاعة اللّه» (best in intellect, most abstinent from Allah's prohibitions, and quickest in obedience to Allah) (14), and in some narrations, as «ازهد فى الدنیا» (most ascetic regarding the world) (15). Just as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Excellence of deed means a deed accompanied by knowledge, sincerity, and piety, not merely a large quantity of deeds. (16)

(10) Anfal, 37. (11) Jathiyah, 22. (12) Anbiya, 104. (13) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 144. (14) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. (15) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 93. (16) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- The universe was created for man. «خلق السموات و الارض ... لیبلوكم»

2- Although Divine power can create everything in an instant, Divine wisdom necessitates that creation be gradual. «خلق ... فى ستة ایّام»

3- The material source of the heavens and the earth was water or molten matter. «كان عرشه على الماء»

4- Water existed before the heavens and the earth. «كان عرشه على الماء»

5- The goal of the creation of all beings was the spiritual and voluntary growth of man. «خلق السموات و الارض ... لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملاً»

6- The quality of the deed is important, not its quantity. «ایّكم احسن عملاً»

7- The heavens and the earth were created for good deeds, not for gratification, pleasure-seeking, or heedlessness. «ایّكم احسن عملاً»

8- Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof; they merely make accusations. «ان هذا الاّ سحرٌ مبین»

Ayah

وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمَّةٍ مَّعْدُودَةٍ لَّيَقُولُنَّ مَا يَحْبِسُهُۥٓ ۗ أَلَا يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمْ لَيْسَ مَصْرُوفًا عَنْهُمْ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۸)
And if We defer their punishment until a certain time, they will surely say, ‘What holds it back?’ Look! On the day it overtakes them it shall not be turned away from them, and they will be besieged by what they used to deride. (8)

Key Points

The word "Ummah" (أُمّة) is used both for a group that shares a common goal and also in the meaning of time. In this Ayah, the intent of "ilā ummatin ma‘dūdah" (إلى أُمّة مَعدودَة) is a specific and limited time. Just as we read in Surah Yusuf, Ayah 45: "wa-ddakara ba‘da ummatin" (وادّكر بعد اُمّةٍ), meaning the released prisoner remembered Yusuf after a period of time. According to some narrations, one of the manifestations (miṣdāq) of "ummatin ma‘dūdah" (أُمّة مَعدودَة) is the time of the uprising of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام, meaning Allah has lifted the punishment from this Ummah until that time.(17) The delay of Divine punishment serves certain interests (maṣāliḥ); such as granting the opportunity for repentance to sinners, the birth of believing children from misguided parents, in addition to the fact that the presence of righteous individuals and their prayers cause a delay in the descent of Divine punishment.

17) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Some of Allah's actions are time-bound. "akhkharnā... ilā ummatin ma‘dūdah" (اخّرنا ... الى امّةٍ معدودة)

2- Allah's wrath can be delayed. "akhkharnā..." (اخّرنا...)

3- The respite and duration of the delay of Divine wrath and punishment are short. "ilā ummatin ma‘dūdah" (الى امّةٍ معدودة)

4- Delay in punishment is a sign of Allah's grace, not a proof of His weakness. Therefore, we should not mock it. "wa la-in akhkharnā... layaqūlanna mā yaḥbisuhū" (و لئن اخّرنا ... لیقولنّ ما یحبسه)

5- Divine wrath will not be averted from the mocking disbelievers. "laysa maṣrūfan ‘anhum" (لیس مصروفاً عنهم)

6- We should not be deceived by Allah's respite; Divine punishment may come sooner or later, but it is inevitable and inescapable. "yawma ya’tīhim laysa maṣrūfan ‘anhum" (یوم یأتیهم لیس مصروفا عنهم)

7- The method of the disbelievers' interaction is mocking religious beliefs. "kānū bihi yastahzi’ūn" (كانوا به یستهزؤن)

Ayah

وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةً ثُمَّ نَزَعْنَـٰهَا مِنْهُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيَـُٔوسٌ كَفُورٌ (۹)
If We let man taste a mercy from Us, and then withdraw it from him, he becomes despondent, ungrateful. (9)

Key Points

Not every blessing is a sign of Allah's love, nor is the removal of every blessing a sign of His wrath and anger; rather, it may well be a divine test. Since man does not know the divine wisdom and the expediency of affairs, he judges quickly, becomes despairing, and commits disbelief (kufr).

Messages

  1. Man's enjoyments are only to the extent of tasting. «اذقنا الناس...»
  2. Divine blessings are Allah's grace and mercy, not our entitlement. «اذقنا... رحمةً»
  3. If the period of man's enjoyment is prolonged, we should not become arrogant. «اذقنا... ثمّ نزعناها» («ثمّ» [thumma] is a sign of a long duration.)
  4. Giving and taking are in His hand. «اذقنا... نزعناها»
  5. The removal of mercy from man is difficult for him. «نزعناها» (Naza' [نَزع] means pulling out accompanied by difficulty.)
  6. Man has little capacity, and by losing a blessing, he despairs of the vast divine mercy. «ثمّ نزعناها ... انّه لیؤس»
  7. Our faith in Allah should not be dependent on the enjoyments and sweetness of life. «نزعناها... انّه لیؤس»
  8. Despair of Allah's mercy is the prelude to disbelief (kufr) and ingratitude.(18) «انّه لیؤسٌ كفور» (18) The Qur'an states in Ayah 87 of Surah Yusuf: «لاییأس من روح اللّه الاّ القوم الكافرون»

Ayah

وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَـٰهُ نَعْمَآءَ بَعْدَ ضَرَّآءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ ذَهَبَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتُ عَنِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَفَرِحٌ فَخُورٌ (۱٠)
And if We let him have a taste of Our blessings after adversities have befallen him, he will surely say, ‘All ills have left me.’ Indeed, he becomes a vain braggart, (10)

Key Points

Time is not always uniform. Rather, according to narrations: «الدَّهر یَومان، یَومٌ لك و یومٌ علیك، فان كان لك فلاتبطر و ان كان عَلیك فاصبر فكلاهما ستُختَبر» (19), Time has two faces: sometimes it is with you and sometimes it is against you. When it is in your favor, do not become arrogant, and when it is against you, adopt patience, for in every situation you are under divine trial. The blessings that reach a person after hardships and difficulties must be a cause for gratitude (shukr) and remembrance (dhikr), not a means for pride and merriment. Two things make joy dangerous: first, a wrong analysis, «ذهب السیئات عنّى» (the evils have departed from me), and second, that this joy causes the humiliation of others and self-glorification. «لفرحٌ فخور»

19) بحار، ج ۷۳، ص ۸۱.

Messages

1- Man has little capacity and boasts with the slightest blessing. «اذقنا... لفرحٌ فخور»

2- The Quran possesses a balanced rhythm and a specific musicality. («نعماء» rhymes with «ضرّاء» and «لیؤس كفور» has the same meter as «لفرح فخور»)

3- Hardships and joys are fleeting. «اذقنا... مسّته»

4- The thought that hardships will no longer afflict a person is a false notion. «ذهب السیئات عنّى»

5- We should not consider successes as a sign of popularity with Allah and say: I will no longer be afflicted! «ذهب السیئات عنّى»

6- Wrong analyses and insights lead to improper behavior. He imagines that no evil remains, «ذهب السیئات عنّى», hence he boasts. «لفرح فخور»

7- The character of some people is unstable and dependent on external events, not internal perfections. Sometimes he is «یؤس كفور» (despairing and ungrateful) and sometimes he is «فرحٌ فخور» (joyful and boastful).

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ (۱۱)
excepting those who are patient and do righteous deeds. For such there will be forgiveness and a great reward. (11)

Key Points

All instances where the Quran speaks of righteous deeds, it is alongside faith, "آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات" (They believed and did righteous deeds), except in this verse where it says: "صبروا و عملوا الصالحات" (They were patient and did righteous deeds). Of course, the intent behind the patient ones in this verse is also the true believers, but because it is presented in contrast to those who lack capacity (or are impatient), the expression "صبروا" (They were patient) is used instead of "آمنوا" (They believed). Patience (ṣabr) is not only in the face of bitterness, but one must also be patient during prosperity and joy, otherwise, it will lead to human transgression. Just as the Children of Israel, when they were freed from Pharaoh's dominion and attained prosperity and freedom, "جعلكم ملوكاً" (He made you kings) (20), they transgressed, turned to calf-worship, and went so far as to attempt to kill the Prophet (Harun) when he forbade the evil. "وكادوا یقتلوننى" (And they nearly killed me) (21).

20) Mā'idah, 20. 21) A‘rāf, 150.

Messages

1- Patience in the face of the bitter and sweet events of life is among the best examples of righteous deeds. "صبروا و عملوا الصالحات"

2- The believer neither despairs nor commits ingratitude or boasts; rather, he is unbreakable and resilient. "صبروا"

3- Patience and steadfastness are means for the forgiveness of sins. "الذین صبروا ... لهم مغفرة"

4- If we know what reward awaits us, patience becomes sweet for us. "الذین صبروا... لهم مغفرة و اجرٌ كبیر"

5- Averting danger is more important than gaining benefit. First "مغفرة" (forgiveness), then "اجر كبیر" (great reward).

Ayah

فَلَعَلَّكَ تَارِكٌۢ بَعْضَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ وَضَآئِقٌۢ بِهِۦ صَدْرُكَ أَن يَقُولُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ كَنزٌ أَوْ جَآءَ مَعَهُۥ مَلَكٌ ۚ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ نَذِيرٌ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَكِيلٌ (۱۲)
[Look out] lest you should disregard aught of what has been revealed to you, and be upset because they say, ‘Why has not a treasure been sent down to him, or [why does] not an angel accompany him?’ You are only a warner, and Allah watches over all things. (12)

Key Points

Sometimes the disbelievers (Kuffar) would ask the Prophet to miraculously turn the mountains of Mecca into gold, or that an angel should descend to confirm him. On the other hand, some, due to malice and hatred, were unwilling for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to state what concerned Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, and even if He did state it, they would not accept it. Therefore, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would feel distressed and uneasy (literally: His chest would become constricted), and consequently, He would delay the message of the verses. This does not contradict the infallibility (Ismah) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. This is because sometimes the conveyance of the Divine message is not immediate, and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would delay conveying the verses based on certain considerations—which were not personal (but rather based on expediency/public interest). However, with the revelation of this Ayah, those considerations are set aside, and the message of the Ayah is conveyed with decisiveness. It is possible that the omission of conveying the revelation was only regarding certain stubborn individuals whose guidance the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله had despaired of. However, Allah says: Although they will not believe, you must not abandon your duty. Perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is that the disbelievers are indifferent and fault-finding people, as if you have not recited Our revelation to them. And perhaps the intent is that the thought of delaying or abandoning the conveyance of some verses of the Quran is causing you distress.

Messages

  1. The preacher must be decisive in conveying the word of Allah and should not heed the words and excuses of the people. «فلعلّك تاركٌ ... ان یقولوا»
  2. In the method of interaction and guidance, instead of saying: "You are such-and-such," we should say: "It seems," "Perhaps," or "Lest you be such-and-such." «فلعلك» instead of «انّك»
  3. In addition to physical harassment and abuse, the disbelievers put pressure on the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله through various excuses. «ضائق به صدرك ان یقولوا ...»
  4. People's intellect is in their eyes; they accept prophethood alongside wealth. «لولا انزل علیه كنزٌ»
  5. We guarantee the duty, not the result. You perform your duty, and entrust the rest of the matters to Allah. «انّما انت نذیر و اللّه على كل شیئ وكیل»
  6. The duty of the preacher is to warn the people (inzar), not to compel them to believe. «انّما انت نذیر»

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِعَشْرِ سُوَرٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ مُفْتَرَيَـٰتٍ وَٱدْعُوا۟ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۳)
Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it?’ Say, ‘Then bring ten surahs like it, fabricated, and invoke whomever you can, besides Allah, should you be truthful.’ (13)

Key Points

The Quran is not a miracle only in terms of eloquence (fasaha) and rhetoric (balagha); rather, it is also a miracle in terms of its knowledge (ma'arif), admonitions (mawa'iz), proofs (barahin), unseen news (akhbar ghaybi), and laws (qawanin). This is because the phrase «وادعوا من استطعتم» (and call upon whomever you can) is a challenge to all people, not just the Arabs who understand the eloquence and rhetoric of the Quran. As He says in another Ayah: «قل لئن اجتمعت الانس و الجنّ على أن یأتوا بمثل هذا القرآن لایأتون بمثله و لوكان بعضهم لبعض ظهیرا» (Say: "If mankind and the jinn were to gather together to produce the like of this Quran, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they were to support one another.") If all mankind and jinn gather, they cannot bring the like of the Quran. (22) The aspects of the Quran's miraculousness (I'jaz) are numerous: the delight and sweetness of its words during recitation; the uniformity of its content despite being revealed over a period of 23 years; the presentation of sciences that were unknown until that time; the prediction of events that later came to pass; the narration of the history of past nations of whom no trace remains; the presentation of comprehensive and complete laws covering all individual and social dimensions of human life; and its preservation from any distortion (tahrif), alteration, obsolescence, or being forgotten throughout the long centuries. Despite all these reductions (in the challenge) and all that incitement, humanity remains incapable. In one place, the Quran says: bring the like of the entire Quran, «ان یأتوا بمثل هذا القرآن » (23). In another place, it says: bring the like of ten Surahs of the Quran (this very Ayah). In yet another place, He reduces the challenge further, saying: bring one Surah like it. «فأتوا بسورةٍ من مثله » (24). In addition to these reductions, He has also employed various types of incitement. In one place, He says: if jinn and mankind support each other, they cannot do it. In another place, He says: invite all the minds on Earth, but you will never be able to bring the like of the Quran. History has also proven that enemies who launched many wars against Islam and plotted conspiracies were never able to bring even one Surah like the Quran. Is a miracle anything other than this?

(22) Isra, 88. (23) Isra, 88. (24) Baqarah, 23.

Messages

1- Instead of presenting proof and evidence, the disbelievers accused the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله of fabricating the Quran, claiming it was his own making, not the Word of God. «یقولون افتریه»

2- The Quran is the eternal miracle of history. «وادعوا من استطعتم»

Ayah

فَإِلَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَكُمْ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَآ أُنزِلَ بِعِلْمِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَن لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۱٤)
But if they do not respond to you, know that it has been sent down by Allah’s knowledge, and that there is no god except Him. Will you, then, submit [to Allah]? (14)

Key Points

The disbelievers used to accuse the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that he learned the Qur'an from someone: «فهى تملى علیه»(25). The Qur'an answers them that the source of this Book is Divine knowledge, not the dictation of others. Sometimes they also said: the Qur'an is his own, but others have helped him. «اعانه علیه قومٌ اخرون»(26). This Ayah is the answer to all those accusations.

25) Al-Furqan, 5. 26) Al-Furqan, 4.

Messages

1- The source of the Qur'an is the infinite knowledge of Allah. «انّما انزل بعلم اللّه»

2- The Qur'an is not a guess, a hypothesis, or human imagination and thought, but rather it is based on the knowledge of Allah. «انزل بعلم اللّه»

3- The Qur'an is not limited to a specific time, place, race, or generation, because it is based on the knowledge of Allah. «انزل بعلم اللّه»

4- The secrets of the Qur'an, like the infinite knowledge of Allah, are boundless. «انزل بعلم اللّه»

5- The disbelief and doubt of the enemies should not cause you to waver regarding the truthfulness of the Qur'an and the Oneness of Allah. «فان لم یستجیبوا ... انزل بعلم اللّه و ان لا اله الاّ هو»

Ayah

مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا نُوَفِّ إِلَيْهِمْ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ فِيهَا وَهُمْ فِيهَا لَا يُبْخَسُونَ (۱٥)
As for those who desire the life of this world and its glitter, We will recompense them fully for their works therein, and they shall not be underpaid in it. (15)

Key Points

| Question: This Ayah states that We will give the result of their deeds to the worldly people completely and without any reduction in this world. However, in other instances, [Allah] says: «من كان یرید العاجلة عجّلنا له فیها ما نشاء لمن نرید»(27) (Whoever desires the immediate [world], We hasten for him therein what We will for whom We intend). We grant [it] to whomever We wish and in whatever amount We wish. How can these verses be reconciled? Answer: In this Ayah, He says: We give the result of their work (اعمالهم), but in that [other] Ayah, the issue is not work, but desire (or aspiration). Allah says that some people achieve a portion of their desires. Those who do not believe in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) or do not work for Allah have no claim upon the Lord. In return for their services, inventions, and discoveries, they achieve fame and sustenance right here in this world. A person can only expect an otherworldly reward if he believes in it. Otherwise, what expectation does someone who accepts neither God nor the Resurrection have from Allah? If a person pursues the world instead of the Hereafter, the transient instead of the eternal, and a limited goal instead of the unlimited, the result of his action will also be limited to this transient world.

27) Isra, 18.

Messages

1- The motive and goal are important, not just the action itself. «من كان یرید الحیاة الدنیا»

2- Man has free will and can intend as he wishes. «من كان یرید»

3- Allah is Just. Even those who do not work for Him, He gives them their reward in this world without any reduction. «نوفّ الیهم ... لایبخسون»

4- The reason for rejecting the truth is worldliness (love of the world). «فان لم یستجیبوا... من كان یرید الحیاةالدنیا»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِلَّا ٱلنَّارُ ۖ وَحَبِطَ مَا صَنَعُوا۟ فِيهَا وَبَـٰطِلٌ مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٦)
They are the ones for whom there shall be nothing in the Hereafter but Fire: what they had accomplished in the world has failed, and their works have come to naught. (16)

Key Points

Where the duty of man was worship and required the intention of proximity (qurbat), the action without the intention of proximity is void (bāṭil). And where the intention of proximity was not necessary, but the action was performed for the sake of this world, its result is limited to this world, and in the Hereafter, that action will be nullified (ḥabṭ) and erased. One of the companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله looked upon a girl and reached out toward her. On the way, a wall collapsed and injured his face. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, upon hearing this incident, said: Allah punished you in this very world so that it would not be carried over to the Hereafter. Just as there are people whose reward for their deeds Allah gives them in this world so that they have no claim in the Hereafter.(28) Imam Ali عليه السلام, while reciting this Ayah, used to say: How can I endure a fire, such that if a spark of it were to fall upon the earth, it would burn all its vegetation.(29)

28) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 29) Bihar, Vol. 40, p. 346.

Messages

1- The hands of those focused on this world will be empty in the Hereafter. «لیس لهم فى الاخرة»

2- Deeds performed for the sake of this world or out of ostentation (riyā') are vain and ruined in the Hereafter. «حبط ماصنعوا فیها و باطلٌ»

Ayah

أَفَمَن كَانَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ وَيَتْلُوهُ شَاهِدٌ مِّنْهُ وَمِن قَبْلِهِۦ كِتَـٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلْأَحْزَابِ فَٱلنَّارُ مَوْعِدُهُۥ ۚ فَلَا تَكُ فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۷)
Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord, and whom a witness of his own [family] follows? And before him there was the Book of Moses, a guide and mercy. It is they who have faith in it, and whoever defies him from among the factions, the Fire is their tryst. So do not be in doubt about it; it is the truth from your Lord, but most people do not have faith. (17)

Key Points

Many narrations have been received from the Infallible Imams علیهم السلام stating that the meaning of "the witness" (شاهد) in this Ayah is Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام. (30) Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام Himself stated in a sermon: The meaning of "the witness" (شاهد) is me. According to this Ayah, the witness (شاهد) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is from him, not separate from him. And according to Shia and Sunni narrations, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: I am from Ali, and Ali is from me. (31) In the last Ayah of Surah Ar-Ra'd, it is mentioned that when the disbelievers said to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: «لست مرسلاً» (You are not a messenger), the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, by the command of Allah, replies: «كفى باللّه شهیداً بینى و بینكم و مَن عنده علم الكتاب» (Sufficient is Allah as a witness between me and you, and he who has knowledge of the Book). Meaning, for witnessing between me and you, Allah and the one who possesses the knowledge of the Book are sufficient. There, too, according to Shia and Sunni narrations, the meaning of «مَن عنده علم الكتاب» (he who has knowledge of the Book) is Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, who is the witness (شهید) and guarantor (گواه) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. He is Shahid (witness/martyr) there, and here He is Shahid (witness).

30) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 190. 31) Sahih Bukhari, Book of Peace, Chapter "How we write"; Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Vol. 5, p. 356.

Messages

  1. In propagation, we should call upon sound consciences for judgment using the method of questioning. «افمن كان»
  2. The Quran is Allah's proof (Hujjah) upon the people. «بینةٍ من ربّه»
  3. The one who accompanies and follows the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله during His lifetime is also the successor (Khalifah) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله after Him. «یتلوه»
  4. The existence of true, devoted, and self-sacrificing followers is one of the ways to recognize the truthfulness and legitimacy of the Prophets. «بیّنة ... شاهدٌ منه»
  5. Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon the people: the Quran itself is a miracle, and He has a witness like Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, and the Torah had previously given glad tidings of Him. «بینةٍ من ربه ... شاهدٌ منه ... و من قبله كتاب موسى »
  6. Heavenly Books confirm one another. «من قبله كتاب موسى »
  7. In propagation, knowing the audience is a fundamental principle, because Jews were more numerous than Christians in Hijaz, the Quran addresses them, although many glad tidings also appeared in the Gospel. «من قبله كتاب موسى »
  8. Heavenly Books and Divine laws are the path to guidance and a means of mercy for the people. «اماماً و رحمة»
  9. People must make the Heavenly Book their Imam and leader. «كتاب موسى اماماً»
  10. Leadership (Imamah) and mercy must be side by side. «اماماً و رحمة»
  11. The criterion and axis must be the truth, not the party. Instead of focusing on party, race, or region, we should seek to follow the truth. «و من یكفر به من الاحزاب»
  12. All people, from every group, tribe, and party, must turn to the Quran, otherwise they will face Divine wrath. «و من یكفر به من الاحزاب فالنار موعده»
  13. The disbelief of some people should not cause discouragement or instability among the believers. «و من یكفر ... فلاتك فى مریةٍ منه»
  14. Majority is not a sign of truthfulness. «الحق من ربّك ... اكثر الناس لایؤمنون»

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ وَيَقُولُ ٱلْأَشْهَـٰدُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ أَلَا لَعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۸)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah? Such shall be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, ‘It is these who lied against their Lord.’ Behold! The curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers (18)

Key Points

The court of Resurrection (Qiyamah) has many witnesses: A: Allah, Who is a witness over all our deeds. «انّ اللّه على كل شیىٍ شهید» (32) B: The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. «فكیف اذا جئنا من كل امّةٍ بشهید و جئنا بك على هولاء شهیداً» (33) How will it be with them on that Day when We bring a witness from every nation and bring you as a witness over them. C: The Infallible Imams علیهم السلام. «و كذلك جعلناكم امّةً وسطاً لتكونوا شهداء على الناس» (34) We made you a middle nation so that you may be witnesses over mankind. According to narrations, the intent of 'nation' (Ummah) here is the Infallible Imams, because other individuals of the nation do not possess the necessary knowledge and infallibility (Ismah) required for witnessing on that Day. D: The Angels. «و جاءت كل نفس معها سائقٌ و شهید» (35) On the Day of Resurrection, two angels come with every soul: one drives him (to the reckoning) and one is a witness over him. E: The Earth. «یومئذ تحدّث اخبارها» (36) On the Day of Resurrection, the earth will recount its news. F: Conscience. «اقرء كتابك كفى بنفسك الیوم علیك حسیباً» (37) Read your book (of deeds); sufficient is your own soul this Day as an accountant against you. G: Body parts. «یوم تشهد علیهم السنتهم و ایدیهم و ارجلهم» (38) On the Day of Resurrection, their tongues, hands, and feet will speak and bear witness against the person. H: Time. Imam Sajjad علیه السلام says in the sixth supplication of the Sahifah (al-Sajjadiyyah): «هذا یومٌ حادثٌ جدید و هو علینا شاهدٌ عتید» Today is a new, fresh day, which will be a ready witness in the Hereafter to the deeds we perform in it. I: Deeds. «وجدوا ما عملوا حاضراً» (39) On the Day of Resurrection, man's deeds will be embodied and appear before him.

Question

The Quran has used the word azlam (most unjust) regarding many sins, while the greatest injustice should be one, not several?

Answer

Although this expression has been used in fifteen instances, all of them relate to intellectual deviations, including polytheism (Shirk), fabrication (slander/calumny), concealing the truth, and hindering the path and remembrance of Allah. Therefore, the greatest injustices are intellectual, cultural, and ideological injustices.

In this Ayah and the subsequent few Ayahs, the Quran describes the portrait and destiny of the person who fabricates lies against Allah as follows: He is the most unjust of people, deprived of Divine favors, does not believe in the Resurrection, distorts the truths, obstructs the path of Allah, has no power to escape Divine dominion in this world, nor any helper in the Hereafter; his punishment is multiplied, his life is wasted, his efforts are ruined, and he has lost his soul and life.

32) Hajj, 17. 33) Nisa, 41. 34) Baqarah, 143. 35) Qaf, 21. 36) Zilzal, 4. 37) Isra, 14. 38) Nur, 24. 39) Kahf, 48.

Messages

1- Fabrication against Allah is the greatest of injustices. Religious writers and speakers must be careful of their speech and writings. «و من اظلم ممن افترى على اللّه»

2- The magnitude and ugliness of injustice depend on its object. «اظلم ممّن افترى على اللّه»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, the veils will be lifted, and nothing will remain hidden. «یعرضون على ربّهم»

4- Many witnesses will testify on the Day of Resurrection, so let us be mindful of our deeds. «و یقول الاشهاد»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۱۹)
—those who bar [others] from the way of Allah, and seek to make it crooked, and disbelieve in the Hereafter. (19)

Key Points

Enemies prevent (people) from the path of Allah through various methods, including: innovation (in religion), slander, interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsir bi al-ra'y), fabrication of hadith, creation of doubts (shubhah), stagnation of mosques, sidelining the Ahl al-Bayt of the Messenger of Allah علیهم السلام, forbidding the good (nahy 'an al-ma'ruf), making excuses, creating unhealthy entertainments, raising secondary issues, personality cults, promoting falsehood, humiliating believers, prohibiting permissible things (tahrim al-mubahat), inappropriate encouragement, wrongful propaganda, and exaggerating the importance of the tyrants (taghuts) and... Among the clear examples of blocking the path of Allah is closing the door of the homes of the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah), the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام, and the infallible and just leaders.

Messages

1- Blocking the path of Allah or showing it to be crooked is oppression (zulm). «القوم الظالمین الذین یصدّون ...»

2- In the first stage, the enemy blocks the path, and if unable to do so, he diverts the course. «یصدّون... و یبغونها عوجاً»

3- Distorting religious knowledge and presenting religion poorly is a type of blocking the path of Allah. «یصدّون... و یبغونها عوجاً»

4- The religion of Allah has no crookedness or deviation whatsoever; the opponents themselves create doubts and deviations. «یبغونها عوجاً»

5- Lack of faith in the Hereafter is the prelude to all forms of oppression. «بالاخرة هم كافرون»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ مُعْجِزِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۘ يُضَـٰعَفُ لَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۚ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يُبْصِرُونَ (۲٠)
They cannot frustrate [Allah] on the earth, nor do they have besides Allah any guardian. For them the punishment shall be doubled, for they could neither listen, nor did they use to see. (20)

Key Points

| Question: How is the doubling of the punishment compatible with Divine Justice? Answer: The person who, due to the position and power he possesses, causes the deviation of others, naturally must also bear their burden (of sin). Therefore, the sin of scholars, due to their exemplary role, is several times greater than that of ordinary individuals, and this is the essence of justice.

Messages

1- Oppressors are under the dominion of Allah's wrath and power, and they will see the consequence of their deeds. «لم یكونوا معجزین فى الارض»

2- Those who fabricate lies should not assume that the support and guardianship of the tyrants (Taghuts) will lead to their salvation. «ما كان لهم من دون اللّه من اولیاء»

3- Misguided leaders who cause the deviation of others also bear the burden of the sins of those people. «یصدّون ... یبغونها عوجاً ... یضاعف لهم العذاب»

4- Disbelief, obstinacy, and fanaticism have so blinded and deafened the eyes and ears of the deviated that they cannot see or hear the truth. «ما كانوا یستطیعون ...»

Ayahs

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۲۱)
They are the ones who have ruined their souls, and what they used to fabricate has forsaken them. (21)
لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمُ ٱلْأَخْسَرُونَ (۲۲)
Undoubtedly, they are the ones who will be the biggest losers in the Hereafter. (22)

Key Points

In the culture of Islam, the world is a market where people are the sellers, and the soul (life), wealth, and deeds are the commodities of this market. The buyers are of two types: the first type is Allah, Who buys at a high price, and the second type is other than Allah, such as Satan and carnal desires (hawa al-nafs), who buy these commodities for a negligible price. Therefore, the terms ajr (reward), thawāb (recompense), ḍi‘f (double), and aḍ‘āf (multiples) are frequently mentioned in the Quran regarding profit, and the terms khusr (loss), khusrān mubīn (manifest loss), and akhsarīn (the greatest losers) are mentioned regarding damage and loss. The important warning is that we must know that every damage and loss is compensable, except the passing of life (time), which never returns. Sometimes a person's wealth and dwelling, or status and power, or certificate and title are lost. But sometimes a person loses himself and wastes his humanity, which is the greatest loss.

Messages

1- False and unjust statements, even if they appear attractive outwardly, are all destined to perish. «ضل عنهم ما كانوا یفترون»

2- The greatest losses are the loss of the self (soul) and one's life (time), not financial losses. «خسروا انفسهم ... هم الاخسرون»

3- Worldly losses are compensable; the important loss is the loss in the Hereafter, for which there is no remedy. «فى الاخرة هُم الاخسرون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَأَخْبَتُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲۳)
Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds and are humble before their Lord—they shall be the inhabitants of paradise, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (23)

Key Points

The word akhbatū is derived from khabt, which has been used both to mean submission and humility, and also used to mean tranquility and assurance. Alongside warning and threat, encouragement has been mentioned. Following the previous verses which described the fate of those who fabricate lies, this verse describes the characteristics of the people of truth.

Messages

1- We should not be arrogant about our faith and righteous deeds, because absolute submission is the condition of servitude. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات و اخبتوا الى ربّهم»

2- Spiritual and heartfelt states are effective in a person's reward. «اخبتوا الى ربّهم»

Ayah

۞ مَثَلُ ٱلْفَرِيقَيْنِ كَٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْأَصَمِّ وَٱلْبَصِيرِ وَٱلسَّمِيعِ ۚ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۲٤)
The parable of the two parties is that of one who is blind and deaf and one who sees and hears. Are they equal in comparison? Will you not then take admonition? (24)

Key Points

Just as the body has eyes and ears, the heart and soul also have eyes and ears. Just as the blind and the deaf do not perceive the sensory world and do not derive pleasure from it, stubborn individuals also do not perceive Divine knowledge and do not derive pleasure from it.

Messages

1- The person whose vision is limited to material sensations and who does not see spirituality and the Hereafter is blind. «مثل الفریقین كالاعمى ... و البصیر»

2- In propagation, we should call upon people's conscience for judgment using the method of questioning. «هل یستویان»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنِّى لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۲٥)
Certainly We sent Noah to his people [to declare]: ‘Indeed I am a manifest warner to you. (25)
أَن لَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ (۲٦)
Worship none but Allah. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.’ (26)

Key Points

Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) is the first of the Prophets of Firm Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) who rose up against polytheism and idol worship. Since the generation of mankind after the drowning of the disbelievers all descended from Noah and his companions, he is therefore called the Second Father (of humanity). And because his lifespan was longer than all other prophets, he has been called "Shaykh al-Anbiya" (The Elder of the Prophets). Perhaps the punishment that Prophet Noah feared would descend upon his people was divine wrath in this very world. As we read in a few subsequent verses, the disbelievers said to Noah: «فأتنا بما تعدنا ان كنت من الصادقین» (Then bring upon us that which you promise us, if you should be of the truthful.)

Messages

1- The scope of the prophets' propagation is primarily their own people. «ارسلنا... الى قومه»

2- The warnings of the prophets are for the benefit of the people. «انّى لكم نذیر»

3- The heedless person needs warning more than anything else. «انّى لكم نذیر»

4- The message and propagation must be clear and illuminating. «نذیرٌ مبین»

5- The prophets both warn and explain the welfare of the people. «نذیرٌ مبین»

6- The goal and duty of the prophets is to articulate the issue of Monotheism (Tawhid). «الاّ تعبدوا الاّ اللّه»

7- The history of polytheism dates back to the time of Prophet Noah as well. «لاتعبدوا الاّ اللّه»

8- The principle of worship and devotion exists in every human being, but the prophets determine its path and direction. «لاتعبدوا الاّ اللّه»

9- The prophets were well-wishers and compassionate toward the people. «انّى اخاف علیكم»

10- The duty of the preacher and educator is to explain the dangers and negative consequences of associating partners with Allah. «اخاف علیكم عذاب یوم الیم»

Ayah

فَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ مَا نَرَىٰكَ إِلَّا بَشَرًا مِّثْلَنَا وَمَا نَرَىٰكَ ٱتَّبَعَكَ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ أَرَاذِلُنَا بَادِىَ ٱلرَّأْىِ وَمَا نَرَىٰ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا مِن فَضْلٍۭ بَلْ نَظُنُّكُمْ كَـٰذِبِينَ (۲۷)
But the elite of the faithless from among his people said, ‘We do not see you to be anything but a human being like ourselves, and we do not see anyone following you except those who are simpleminded riffraff from our midst. Nor do we see that you have any merit over us. Indeed, we consider you to be liars.’ (27)

Key Points

Opponents of the Truth sometimes weakened the divine leaders, saying: "You are human beings just like us, not superior to us." Sometimes they belittled their followers, saying: "They are worthless and insignificant individuals." And sometimes they weakened the school of thought (the message), saying: "Your speech is a lie and slander, or magic, or tales of the ancients, or your words are ordinary statements which, if we wished, we could also produce the like of."

Messages

1- Usually, the elite (aristocrats) were in the front line of opposition to the prophets. «فقال الملاء الذین كفروا»

2- The arrogant are displeased with equality with the masses, especially the deprived. «هم اراذلنا»

3- Those who have less attachment to the world embrace faith better and sooner. «مانراك اتّبعك الاّ الذین هم اراذلنا»

4- In the view of the worldly, wealth and riches are value, not truth and reality. «ما نرى لكم علینا من فضل»

5- Opponents of the prophets lack proof (evidence), and what they say is based on conjecture, suspicion, and illusion. «بل نظنّكم»

6- The intellect of the disbelievers is in their eyes. The phrase «ما نرى» (We do not see) is mentioned 3 times in this Ayah.

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَءَاتَىٰنِى رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِهِۦ فَعُمِّيَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنُلْزِمُكُمُوهَا وَأَنتُمْ لَهَا كَـٰرِهُونَ (۲۸)
He said, ‘O my people! Tell me, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has granted me His own mercy—though it should be invisible to you—shall we force it upon you while you are averse to it? (28)

Key Points

This Ayah responds to two objections raised by the opponents in the previous Ayah: A: The disbelievers used to say: You are a human being just like us. The Ayah responds: Although I am outwardly like you, I have been favored with the special grace and mercy of the Lord, and revelation (Wahy) is sent down to me. B: The opponents used to say: Your followers are simple-minded and shallow people. The Ayah responds that this is not the case. Although they may appear weaker than you outwardly, they believed upon seeing the clear proof (Bayyinah) and evidence, not without reason. The Prophets possess forbearance (Sa'at al-Sadr) when facing their opponents; despite all the inappropriate remarks and baseless accusations, they still speak of logic, evidence, and compassion.

Messages

1- The Prophets were kind, among the people, and towards the people. «قال یا قوم» 2- In the method of propagation, we should use the leverage of emotion/compassion. «یا قوم» 3- The call of the Prophets was accompanied by clear proof (Bayyinah) and evidence. «كنت على بیّنةٍ» 4- The station of prophethood is a Divine mercy. «رحمةً من عنده» 5 - The Prophets attribute all their perfections to Allah. «من ربّى، من عنده» 6- Whoever cannot perceive the evidence, spirituality, and prophethood is blind. «فعمّیت علیكم» 7- Man has free choice in selecting his religion and creed. «انلزمكموها» 8 - Unless a person possesses the readiness to accept the truth, he cannot benefit from the light of revelation. «انتم لها كارهون»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ لَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مَالًا ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِطَارِدِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَـٰكِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُمْ قَوْمًا تَجْهَلُونَ (۲۹)
O my people! I do not ask you any material reward for it. My reward lies only with Allah. But I will not drive away those who have faith: they will indeed encounter their Lord. But I see that you are an ignorant lot. (29)

Messages

1- The lack of expectation (of reward) by the Prophets is one of the signs of their truthfulness. «لااسئلكم علیه مالاً»

2- The secret to a preacher's success is having no expectations from the people and disregarding the wealthy. «لااسئلكم علیه مالاً» Of course, religious preachers have a great reward with Allah. «ان اجرى الا على اللّه»

3- If the Prophets were seeking exploitation, they would have approached the elite, not the poor. «لااسئلكم علیه مالاً»

4- The elite conditioned their belief on the poor being driven away, but the Prophets firmly opposed this request. «ما أنا بطارد...» (The nominal sentence structure and the particle bā’ (باء) indicate decisiveness/firmness.)

5- For the one whose goal in preaching is neither wealth nor status, the poor and the rich are equal in his sight. «ما انا بطارد الذین امنوا»

6- We should not pay attention to the expectations of the arrogant. «ما انا بطارد الذین امنوا»

7- The disbelievers considered the believers to be riff-raff (lowly), but the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: I will not exchange them for you. «ما انا بطارد الذین امنوا»

8- A righteous government should not lose the deprived class merely to gain the satisfaction of the affluent. «ما انا بطارد الذین امنوا»

9- Those who have honor (standing) with Allah should not be driven away. «ما انا بطارد الذین امنوا»

10- Remembering the Day of Judgment gives the correct direction to worldly stances. «ملاقواربّهم»

11- Humiliating believers because of their poverty is a sign of foolishness. «قوم تجهلون»

12- The Quran identifies those who consider believers to be riff-raff as ignorant and foolish. «قوم تجهلون» (Neither aristocracy is a sign of understanding, nor is poverty a sign of ignorance.)

13- One of the degrees of Irfan (spiritual knowledge) is meeting (Allah) and witnessing (Him), a station of which the affluent are unaware. «الّذین آمنوا انّهم ملاقوا ربّهم ولكنّى اراكم قوماً تجهلون»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ مَن يَنصُرُنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِن طَرَدتُّهُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۳٠)
O my people! Who would come to my help against Allah were I to drive them away? Will you not then take admonition? (30)

Messages

1- Rejecting and humiliating believing people (even by the prophets) will result in Divine wrath. «من ینصرنى من اللّه ان طردتهم»

2- No one can prevent the wrath of Allah. «من ینصرنى من اللّه»

3- By rejecting the believers, who will come to the aid of God's religion? «من ینصرنى»

4- Let us not trade the satisfaction of the creation for the wrath of the Creator. «من ینصرنى من اللّه ان طردتهم»

5- The prophets' maintenance of good relations with people was based on Divine duty, not for the sake of their personal or social status. «من ینصرنى من اللّه»

6- In propagation, use the method of questioning to call people's conscience and thought to judgment. «من ینصرنى، افلا تذكّرون»

7- Everyone acknowledges the powerlessness of all beings before Divine wrath, even though they sometimes become heedless of it. «افلا تذكّرون»

Ayah

وَلَآ أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِندِى خَزَآئِنُ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَعْلَمُ ٱلْغَيْبَ وَلَآ أَقُولُ إِنِّى مَلَكٌ وَلَآ أَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ تَزْدَرِىٓ أَعْيُنُكُمْ لَن يُؤْتِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ خَيْرًا ۖ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ إِنِّىٓ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۳۱)
I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, neither do I know the Unseen. I do not claim to be an angel, neither do I say of those who are despicable in your eyes that Allah will not grant them any good—Allah knows best what is in their hearts—for then I would indeed be a wrongdoer.’ (31)

Key Points

Absolute knowledge of the Unseen (Ghayb), concerning everything and under all circumstances, belongs exclusively to Allah. «و عنده مفاتح الغیب لایعلمها الاّ هو» (40) However, by the permission and will of Allah, sometimes windows to the Unseen are opened for His chosen servants (Awliya'). «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك» (41) Therefore, the Divine Prophets possess knowledge of the Unseen. As He states in Ayah 26 of Surah Al-Jinn: «عالم الغیب فلایظهر على غیبه احداً الاّ مَن ارتضى من رسول» Not only the knowledge of the Unseen, but also the power to perform extraordinary deeds (miracles) belongs exclusively to Allah. However, Allah grants it to whomever He wills and to the extent He deems appropriate. Although death and life are in His hand, «انّه هو أمات و احیى » (42), yet Jesus (Isa) عليه السلام also revives the dead by His permission, «احيى الموتى باذن اللّه» (43). Although Allah takes the souls, «اللّه یتوفّى الانفس» (44), yet the angels also take the lives of people by His permission, «یتوفّاكم ملك الموت» (45). Although He alone is the Creator, «اللّه خالق كل شیئ» (46), yet Jesus also created by Divine permission. «و اذ تخلق من الطین كهیئة الطیر باذنى» (47).

40) An‘am, 59. 41) Hud, 49. 42) Najm, 44. 43) Aal-i Imran, 49. 44) Zumar, 42. 45) Sajdah, 11. 46) Ra‘d, 16. 47) Ma’idah, 110.

Messages

1 - Prophets do not make empty claims; their work is invitation (Da'wah) and their goal is guidance, all within the framework of servitude to Allah. «و لااقول لكم ...»

2 - The condition for prophethood is not the knowledge of the Unseen or possessing the treasures of existence. «و لااقول لكم عندى خزائن اللّه...»

3 - The goal of the Prophets is spiritual guidance, not the distribution of material treasures. «و لااقول لكم عندى خزائن اللّه...»

4 - Every blessing that reaches us has a source and a treasury. (The word «خزائن» is plural and refers to the source of all blessings.)

5 - We should not exaggerate (commit Ghuluw) regarding the chosen servants of Allah. «و لااقول لكم عندى خزائن اللّه ولا... ولا...»

6 - The nobles of Noah’s people were superficial (judging by appearances). «تزدرى اعینكم...»

7 - When judging others, we should not look at their outward appearance. «تزدرى اعینكم...»

8 - Allah’s favors will be granted to the unknown and weak believers, regardless of our imagination or delusion. «تزدرى اعینكم لن یؤتیهم اللّه خیرا اللّه اعلم بما فى انفسهم»

9 - The criterion for Allah’s favor is the inner and spiritual worthiness of the person. «اللّه اعلم بمافى انفسهم»

10 - Rejecting and deeming the deprived unworthy merely because of poverty is oppression (Zulm). «انّى اذاً لمن الظالمین»

11 - Empty and hollow claims and the misuse of false titles and positions is oppression (Zulm). «انّى اذاً لمن الظالمین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰنُوحُ قَدْ جَـٰدَلْتَنَا فَأَكْثَرْتَ جِدَٰلَنَا فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۳۲)
They said, ‘O Noah, you have disputed with us already, and you have disputed with us exceedingly. Now bring us what you threaten us with, should you be truthful. (32)

Key Points

Argumentation (Mujadalah), if it is based on truth, is a value, «و جادلهم بالّتى هى احسن»(48), and if it is based on falsehood, it is an anti-value. «و جادلوا بالباطل لیدحضوا به الحق»(49)

48) Nahl, 126. 49) Mu'min, 5.

Messages

1- Prophets are steadfast and persistent in their call. «جادلتنا فاكثرت جدالنا»

2- The enemy labels the logic and proof of truth as contention and strife. «قد جادلتنا»

3- We should not consider Allah's respite a sign of the correctness of our path. «فأتنا بما تعدنا»

4- Man is impatient even regarding the descent of punishment upon himself. «فأتنا بما تعدنا»

5- The curse of Prophet Noah عليه السلام followed the declaration by the disbelievers of their readiness for destruction. «فأتنا بما تعدنا»

6- Since the disbelievers lack logic and do not accept logical discourse, they propose the termination of negotiations (by demanding the punishment). «فأتنا بما تعدنا»

7- Noah continuously promised the disbelievers punishment. «تعدنا» instead of «وعدتنا»

8- The disbelievers either doubted the sincerity of the Prophets or expressed doubt in their speech. «ان كنت من الصادقین»

Ayahs

قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَأْتِيكُم بِهِ ٱللَّهُ إِن شَآءَ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (۳۳)
He said, ‘Allah will indeed bring it on you if He wishes, and you cannot frustrate [Him]. (33)
وَلَا يَنفَعُكُمْ نُصْحِىٓ إِنْ أَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَنصَحَ لَكُمْ إِن كَانَ ٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَن يُغْوِيَكُمْ ۚ هُوَ رَبُّكُمْ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۳٤)
My exhorting will not benefit you, much as I may seek to exhort you, if Allah desires to consign you to perversity. He is your Lord, and to Him you shall be brought back.’ (34)

Key Points

The word nush (نُصح) refers to an action or speech intended for reform and done with sincerity. And the word ghayy (غَىّ) refers to ignorance resulting from corrupt belief. The difference between idhlāl (اضلال - leading astray) and ighwā’ (اغواء - seduction/misguidance) is that the dhāll (the one astray) knows the goal but has lost the path, whereas in ighwā’, both the destination and the goal are lost. Attributing idhlāl and ighwā’ to Allah is due to the punishment for the obstinacy and moral corruption of the misguided, not that Allah misguides anyone initially and without prerequisite. «و ما یضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین» (50)

50) Al-Baqarah, 26.

Messages

1- The Prophet is commissioned to convey the message, and the retribution and punishment of the misguided are in the hands of Allah. «انّما یأتیكم به اللّه»

2- Nothing compels Allah to do anything (even after His own threats). «ان شاء»

3- The disbelievers can neither prevent the descent of the punishment nor can they escape from it. «ما انتم بمعجزین»

4- Prophets are compassionate toward people. «نصحى»

5- The Will of Allah prevails over all other wills. «و لاینفعكم نصحى ... ان كان اللّه یرید ان یغویكم» (Even the effectiveness of the Prophet's fervor, speech, and advice is by His Will.)

6- The guidance or misguidance of people is not outside the scope of the Divine Will. «یرید ان یغویكم»

7- The misguidance (ighwā’) of the astray, following their obstinacy and demand for punishment, «فأتنا بما تعدنا», is a manifestation of Divine Lordship. «ان یغویكم هو ربّكم»

8- Rejecting the advice of the Prophets leads to Divine wrath. «لاینفعكم نصحى... الیه ترجعون»

9- Even if we are not punished in this world, our ultimate reckoning in the Hereafter is with Allah. «هو ربّكم و الیه ترجعون»

10- The return to Allah is compulsory. «الیه تُرجعون» (passive voice) not «تَرجعون» (active voice).

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ إِنِ ٱفْتَرَيْتُهُۥ فَعَلَىَّ إِجْرَامِى وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُجْرِمُونَ (۳٥)
Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it?’ Say, ‘Should I have fabricated it, then my guilt will be upon me, and I am absolved of your guilty conduct.’ (35)

Key Points

The words ijrām (اِجرام) and jurm (جُرم) linguistically mean picking unripe fruit, and subsequently, they have been applied to every undesirable act and transgression.

Messages

1- Allah teaches the Prophet the method of questioning, answering, and dealing with the polytheists. «یقولون... قل...»

2- Attributing fabricated statements to Allah is a crime and a sin. «ان افتریته فعلىّ اجرامى»

3- Everyone is responsible for his own actions. «ان افتریته فعلىّ اجرامى»

4- One must disavow polytheism (Shirk). «و أنا برى ء»

5- Polytheism (Shirk) is a crime. «ممّا تجرمون»

Ayah

وَأُوحِىَ إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ أَنَّهُۥ لَن يُؤْمِنَ مِن قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَن قَدْ ءَامَنَ فَلَا تَبْتَئِسْ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ (۳٦)
It was revealed to Noah: ‘None of your people will believe except those who already have faith; so do not sorrow for what they used to do. (36)

Key Points

It is inferred from this Ayah that the curse of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام concerning his people, when he said: «ربّ لاتذر على الارض من الكافرین دیّاراً»(51) (O Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers upon the earth), was based on the information that Allah had given Him according to this blessed Ayah.

51) Nuh, 27.

Messages

1- Man sometimes falls to a stage where there is no hope for his salvation. «لن یؤمن من قومك»

2- Allah places knowledge of future events at the disposal of the Prophets. «اوحى الى نوح انّه لن یؤمن»

3- Religious leaders and preachers should not expect all people to respond positively to them. «لن نؤمن من قومك»

4- Although the Prophets are compassionate towards people, one should not grieve over obstinate individuals. «فلاتبتئس»

Ayah

وَٱصْنَعِ ٱلْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا وَوَحْيِنَا وَلَا تُخَـٰطِبْنِى فِى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُم مُّغْرَقُونَ (۳۷)
Build the ark before Our eyes and by Our revelation, and do not plead with Me for those who are wrongdoers: they shall indeed be drowned.’ (37)

Messages

1- Allah realizes His will through natural means and causes, and one should not always wait for supernatural matters. «واصنع الفلك»

2- Working is not shameful for the friends of Allah. «واصنع الفلك»

3- For the salvation of people, lecturing and propagation are not sufficient; the means of rescue must also be provided. «واصنع الفلك»

4- Shipbuilding has a very long history. Noah was addressed: «اِصنع الفلك»

5- Attention to the presence of Allah gives a person vitality and encouragement, making him resistant against adversities. «واصنع الفلك باعیننا»

6- Human industrial innovations and inventions stem from Divine inspirations. «و اصنع... باعیننا و وحینا»

7- The intercession of the Prophets is ineffective concerning the oppressive disbelievers. «و لا تخاطبنى فى الذین ظلموا»

8- Obstinacy against the Prophets is oppression. «فى الذین ظلموا»

9- The prayer and intercession of the Prophets are ineffective regarding Divine decrees. «ولاتخاطبنى... انّهم مغرقون»

10- Oppressors are exposed to being afflicted by worldly punishments. «انّهم مغرقون»

Ayah

وَيَصْنَعُ ٱلْفُلْكَ وَكُلَّمَا مَرَّ عَلَيْهِ مَلَأٌ مِّن قَوْمِهِۦ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُ ۚ قَالَ إِن تَسْخَرُوا۟ مِنَّا فَإِنَّا نَسْخَرُ مِنكُمْ كَمَا تَسْخَرُونَ (۳۸)
As he was building the ark, whenever the elders of his people passed by him, they would ridicule him. He said, ‘If you ridicule us [today], we shall ridicule you [tomorrow] just as you ridicule us [now]. (38)

Key Points

We should not allow weakness to creep into us due to the mockery of enemies, for the Prophets were also subjected to ridicule. When Prophet Nuh (Noah) began building the Ark, one person would say: His prophethood didn't work out, so He took up carpentry! Another would say: You are building a ship in the middle of dry land, where will the sea come from? Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Prophet Nuh عليه السلام built His Ark over a period of eighty years. (52) The location where the Ark was built was the Mosque of Kufa (53), and the length of the Ark was approximately 400 meters, and its width was approximately 40 meters. (54)

52) Tafsir Burhan. 53) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 54) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Prophets are submissive to the command of Allah. «اصنع الفلك ... یصنع الفلك»

2- Prophet Nuh's shipyard was located in a place where people passed by. «مرّ علیه»

3- The enemy's campaign of propaganda and harassment is comprehensive, constant, and widespread. «كلّمامرّ»

4- If we do not understand the wisdom behind an action (especially the actions of the Prophets), we should not mock it. «كلّما مرّ علیه... سخروا منه»

5- An enemy who lacks logic and proof resorts to mocking and ridiculing the believers. «سخروا منه»

6- We should not neglect reforms and preparations for future generations due to public pressure and gossip. «و یصنع الفلك ... سخروا منه»

7- The followers of Nuh assisted Him in building the Ark. «ان تسخروا منّا فانّا نسخر منكم»

8- Although the enemies addressed Nuh personally, their goal was to ridicule the religion of Nuh and His followers. «سخروا منه ... تسخروا منّا»

9- Initiating mockery is ugly, but mocking the mocker as a punishment and retaliation is pure justice. (55) «ان تسخروا منّا فانّا نسخر منكم»

10- We should not lose heart in the face of the enemies' mockery; rather, we should react. «ان تسخروا منّا فانّا نسخر منكم» 55) As the Quran states: «جزاء سیئة سیئة مثلها» Shura, 40.

Ayah

فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَيَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ (۳۹)
Soon you will know whom a disgraceful punishment will overtake and on whom a lasting punishment will descend.’ (39)

Key Points

According to Tafsir Al-Mizan, the meaning of «عذابٌ یخزیه» is drowning in the world (worldly disgrace), and the meaning of «عذابٌ مقیم» is the punishment of the Day of Judgment.

Messages

1- Through warning and threat, we should take away the sweetness of mockery from the disbelievers. «فسوف تعلمون»

2- We should compare the bitterness of being mocked in the way of Allah with the enduring punishment of the disbelievers and endure it. «یحل علیه عذابٌ مقیم»

3- The punishments of the Hereafter for the mocking disbelievers are enduring. «عذابٌ مقیم»

Ayah

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا وَفَارَ ٱلتَّنُّورُ قُلْنَا ٱحْمِلْ فِيهَا مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَوْلُ وَمَنْ ءَامَنَ ۚ وَمَآ ءَامَنَ مَعَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ (٤٠)
When Our edict came and the oven gushed [a stream of water], We said, ‘Carry in it a pair of every kind [of animal], along with your family—except those [of them] against whom the edict has already been given—and those who have faith.’ And none believed with him except a few. (40)

Key Points

The meaning of al-tannūr (التنور) in the Ayah is the same as the Persian tannūr (oven/furnace). The meaning of the water gushing forth from the tannūr is either the intensity of Divine Wrath, such that water boiled even from an oven which is a place for fire, or it refers to a specific oven from which the boiling of water was a sign of the commencement of Divine punishment. (56)

According to some narrations, Prophet Noah (عليه السلام), besides the animals, also took trees with him onto the Ark (57). This point is consistent with the Ayah, "مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ" (from every pair, two), meaning one pair of every creature, considering that pairing (being male and female) is not exclusive to animals. However, in one narration, we read that the intent of "مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ" is to take two pairs of every male and female (i.e., a male and female domestic animal, and a male and female wild animal) onto the Ark. (58)

According to Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani, Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) had four sons: Canaan, who perished; Sam, the father of the Arabs; Ham, the father of the Sudanese; and Japheth, the father of the Turks. A narration states that Noah had a believing daughter who boarded the Ark with him. (59)

It is mentioned in a Hadith that when Allah intended to punish the people of Noah, He made the women barren for forty years so that no child would be born. (60)

56) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 57) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 58) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 59) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 60) Bihar, Vol. 11, p. 312

Messages

1- The mockery by the disbelievers continued until the moment Divine Wrath arrived. "حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَ أَمْرُنَا"

2- The story of Prophet Noah was extremely important. "أَمْرُنَا"

3- Every kind of boiling and gushing forth of water is under Divine command. "جَاءَ أَمْرُنَا وَ فَارَ التَّنُّورُ"

4- Allah is both the Creator of means and the Nullifier of means (He can establish causes and negate their effects). He makes the oven of fire a place for water to gush forth, just as He made the fire a garden of roses for Abraham (عليه السلام). Water, which is the source of life, becomes the cause of destruction and the extinction of a generation. "فَارَ التَّنُّورُ"

5- The lineage of animals and living creatures must be preserved. "احْمِلْ فِيهَا مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ"

6- Principle, not relationship. Ideological ties supersede family relationships. Noah's wife and son did not have the right to board the Ark. "إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ الْقَوْلُ"

7- The followers of the Truth have been few throughout history. "وَمَا آمَنَ مَعَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ"

In a narration, the meaning of qalīl (قليل - few) is stated to be eighty people who believed in Noah. (61) 61) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

۞ وَقَالَ ٱرْكَبُوا۟ فِيهَا بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ مَجْر۪ىٰهَا وَمُرْسَىٰهَآ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٤۱)
He said, ‘Board it: In the Name of Allah it shall set sail and cast anchor. Indeed my Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (41)

Key Points

Abu Dharr, the great Companion of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, while holding the ring of the door of the Ka'bah, was saying in a loud voice: O people! I heard with my own two ears that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَثل اهل بیتى كمثل سفینة نوح، مَن رَكبها نَجى و مَن تَخلّف عنها هلك» (The similitude of my Ahl al-Bayt is like the Ark of Noah; whoever boards it is saved, and whoever stays away from it is destroyed.) My Ahl al-Bayt are like the Ark of Noah; whoever boards it is saved, and whoever stays away from it is destroyed. Of course, this hadith of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله has also been narrated by other great figures of early Islam, such as Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, Ibn Abbas, Abdullah ibn Zubayr, and Anas ibn Malik, and it is recorded in the well-known books of the Ahl al-Sunnah. (62) If we place this mutawatir (continuously narrated) hadith alongside another mutawatir hadith in which the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله stated that Muslims would divide into 73 sects, and only one sect among them would be saved, «فرفةٌ ناجیة» (the saved sect), it becomes clear that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله Himself introduced His Ahl al-Bayt as the embodiment of the saved sect, saying: «مَن ركبها نجى» (whoever boards it is saved), where the word najā (نجى) interprets the word nājiyah (ناجیه). It is mentioned in narrations that the start of the Ark's journey was on the first of Rajab (63) and began from the Mosque of Kufa. (64)

62) Tabari in Mu'jam al-Kabir, Hakim Nishapuri in Mustadrak, Ibn Kathir in his Tafsir, Suyuti in Tarikh al-Khulafa, Ibn Qutaybah in Uyun al-Akhbar, Tabari in Dhakha'ir al-Uqba, Khatib al-Baghdadi in Tarikh Baghdad, Abu Nu'aym in Hilyat al-Awliya, Ibn Abi al-Hadid in Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah, Alusi in Ruh al-Ma'ani, and other books, the detailed explanation of which is found in Ihqāq al-Haqq, Volume 9, page 270 onwards. 63) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 64) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 492.

Messages

1- Starting any task with the Name of Allah is the secret to reliance (tawakkul), seeking aid, and giving that task a divine color. «اركبوا فیها بسم اللّه»

2- Movement and stopping must both be done by seeking aid from God. «بسم اللّه مجریها ومرسیها»

3- The Ark is merely a means; man must remember God, for everything is by His will. «اركبوا فیها بسم اللّه»

4- The salvation of Noah's followers from the flood is a reflection of divine Lordship (Rububiyyah), forgiveness, and mercy. «اركبوا... انّ ربّى لغفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَهِىَ تَجْرِى بِهِمْ فِى مَوْجٍ كَٱلْجِبَالِ وَنَادَىٰ نُوحٌ ٱبْنَهُۥ وَكَانَ فِى مَعْزِلٍ يَـٰبُنَىَّ ٱرْكَب مَّعَنَا وَلَا تَكُن مَّعَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٤۲)
And it sailed along with them amid waves [rising] like mountains. Noah called out to his son, who stood aloof, ‘O my son! ‘Board with us, and do not be with the faithless!’ (42)

Key Points

Apparently, the conversation between Prophet Noah and his son took place before the ship started moving, because after it started, especially amidst waves like mountains, boarding would be impossible. (65) Question: Why did Noah invite only his son to board? Answer: Firstly: A person has a heavier responsibility towards his own child. Secondly: Because the son had moved aside, Noah thought he had regretted his actions and that a readiness to accept the truth had appeared in him. It is also possible that this was a warning to others that the punishment was serious, since he was inviting his own son to salvation.
