Prophet Noah fasted on the day he entered the ship, and He also commanded His companions to fast, saying: Fasting is the secret to salvation from dangers. (66) Passing through waves like mountains is a sign of the vastness and greatness of Noah's Ark and the skill and expertise of Noah, the Prophet of Allah.

65) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 66) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Even those who are saved have a turbulent path; boarding the ship with a captain like Noah does not mean the task is finished. «فى موجٍ كالجبال»

2- We must think about saving others until the last moment, even if they are disbelievers. «نادى نوحٌ ابنه»

3- Parents are responsible for the destiny of their children. «یا بنىّ اركب معنا»

4- Association with disbelievers separates even the son of a Prophet from the true religion. «لاتكن مع الكافرین» (Do not be with the disbelievers). Sa'di says regarding this:

The son of Noah sat with the wicked, His lineage of prophethood was lost.

5- A sound family alone is not enough; an environment steeped in disbelief causes many deviations. «لا تكن مع الكافرین »

Ayah

قَالَ سَـَٔاوِىٓ إِلَىٰ جَبَلٍ يَعْصِمُنِى مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ ۚ قَالَ لَا عَاصِمَ ٱلْيَوْمَ مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ ۚ وَحَالَ بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلْمَوْجُ فَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُغْرَقِينَ (٤۳)
He said, ‘I shall take refuge on a mountain; it will protect me from the flood.’ He said, ‘There is none today who can protect from Allah’s edict, except someone upon whom He has mercy.’ Then the waves came between them, and he was among those who were drowned. (43)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: In the incident of Prophet Noah عليه السلام, the water covered the entire world, except for the House of Allah, the Ka'bah. (67)

67) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- During dangers and difficulties, the monotheist seeks refuge in Allah, while the polytheist seeks refuge in the mountain. Relying on the East, the West, wealth, or status is polytheism (Shirk). «ساوى الى جبل»

2- If the wrath of Allah arrives, strong and firm mountains cannot provide salvation against soft and flowing water. «جبل یعصمنى من الماء قال لاعاصم الیوم من امراللّه»

3- Water, which is the source of life for all living creatures, becomes the source of destruction and punishment by Divine command. «لاعاصم الیوم من امراللّه»

4- Natural occurrences are not accidents; they are the wise command of Allah. «امراللّه»

5- One must seek refuge from the wrath of Allah only in Him. (68) «لاعاصم الیوم... الاّ من رحم»

6- The result of being «مع الكافرین» (with the disbelievers) is becoming «مِن المغرقین» (among the drowned).

7- In Divine punishment, family and kinship ties have no effect. The disobedient son of the Prophet of Allah drowns before his father, and the father must submit. «حال بینهما الموج و كان من المغرقین»

8- Although Allah is Merciful (Rahim), He is also Wise (Hakim). Sometimes, out of Mercy, He fills the mother's breast with milk before the child's birth, and sometimes, out of Wisdom, He destroys the child before the father's eyes. «من المغرقین »

68) As we read in the Supplication of Abu Hamzah al-Thumali: «هاربٌ منك الیك» (Fleeing from You to You).

Ayah

وَقِيلَ يَـٰٓأَرْضُ ٱبْلَعِى مَآءَكِ وَيَـٰسَمَآءُ أَقْلِعِى وَغِيضَ ٱلْمَآءُ وَقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ وَٱسْتَوَتْ عَلَى ٱلْجُودِىِّ ۖ وَقِيلَ بُعْدًا لِّلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤٤)
Then it was said, ‘O earth, swallow your water! O sky, leave off!’ The waters receded; the edict was carried out, and it settled on [Mount] Judi. Then it was said, ‘Away with the wrongdoing lot!’ (44)

Key Points

This Ayah is one of the most eloquent and expressive verses of the Quran, which has compelled linguists and scholars of rhetoric to praise and wonder from the time of the Prophet until now. Those who intended to challenge the Quran and produce a discourse like it abandoned their efforts when they reached this Ayah. Bal‘ (بَلع) means to swallow down (subside), Iqlā‘ (اِقلاع) means to hold back (cease), and Gheeḍ (غِیض) means the absorption and reduction of water. The meaning of Jūdī (جُودى) is either a general term for a hard mountain or the name of a specific mountain whose location is disputed among the following regions: a: Mosul, Iraq. b: The vicinity of Sham (Syria). c: Between Mosul and Al-Jazira. d: Arabia. e: A mountain in the Ararat mountain range in Turkey. It is mentioned in narrations that Noah's Ark remained on the water for six months and traveled through the land of Mecca and Hijaz until finally, by the command of Allah, the water subsided.(69) And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The day Noah's Ark rested upon the mountain was the day of Nowruz (New Year).(70)

69) According to a hadith in Tahdhib al-Ahkam, the water subsided at the point where the Kufa Mosque stands today. 70) Bihar, Vol. 11, p. 342.

Messages

1- Even after the destruction (of the oppressors), the oppressor must be cursed. «بُعداً للقوم الظالمین»

Ayah

وَنَادَىٰ نُوحٌ رَّبَّهُۥ فَقَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ ٱبْنِى مِنْ أَهْلِى وَإِنَّ وَعْدَكَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَأَنتَ أَحْكَمُ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ (٤٥)
Noah called out to his Lord, and said, ‘My Lord! My son is indeed from my family. Your promise is indeed true, and You are the fairest of all judges.’ (45)

Key Points

Since Allah had promised the salvation of Noah's family (in accordance with Ayah 40), and Noah thought that only his wife was condemned to destruction, it was natural that regarding his son, he would both invite him to board and request his salvation from Him.

Messages

1- In supplication and speaking with Allah, we must observe proper etiquette. Prophet Noah did not say: O God, according to the promise You gave that You would save my family, You should have saved my son, why did You not save him?! Rather, he said: «انت احكم الحاكمین»

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰنُوحُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيْسَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ عَمَلٌ غَيْرُ صَـٰلِحٍ ۖ فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِ مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَعِظُكَ أَن تَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (٤٦)
Said He, ‘O Noah! Indeed, He is not of your family. Indeed, he is [personification of] unrighteous conduct. So do not ask Me [something] of which you have no knowledge. I advise you lest you should be among the ignorant.’ (46)

Key Points

The intent of the phrase "فلاتسئلن" (Do not ask) regarding the infallible Prophet (Noah) is not that a question had already occurred, because Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) says in the next Ayah: "انّى اعوذ بك ان اسئلك ما لیس لى به علم" (Indeed, I seek refuge in You from asking You that of which I have no knowledge). Rather, the meaning is: beware lest you ask! However, the issue that arose for Noah was that he imagined his son was among his family (worthy of salvation), and Allah told him: He is not of your family. Many narrations have reached us from the Prophet and the Imams (علیهم السلام) which begin with the phrase: "لیس منّا" (He is not of us). A few examples are mentioned below: (71) The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "مَن غَشّ مسلماً فلیس منّا" (Whoever cheats a Muslim is not of us). In another narration, He said: "مَن اكرمه الناس اتّقاء شرّه فلیس منّى" (He whom people honor out of fear of his evil is not of me). In another Hadith, the Prophet said: "مَن اصبح و لم یهتمّ بامور المسلمین فلیس بِمسلم" (Whoever wakes up and is not concerned with the affairs of the Muslims is not a Muslim).

Question: In Ayah 10 of Surah At-Tahrim, regarding the wives of Noah and Lot, we read: "خانتا" (They betrayed). And here Allah says: O Noah! Your son is not of your family. Does it follow from these two Ayahs that Noah's son was born out of wedlock (illegitimate)?

Answer: Firstly: The intent of the betrayal by Noah's wife was the disclosure of secrets and aiding the disbelievers, not anything else. Secondly: This Ayah considers the reason for Noah's son not being of his family to be the son's unrighteous deed ("عملِ غیر صالح"), and nothing else.

(71) Most of these narrations are found in Safinat al-Bihar, Volume 2, pages 318 and 691.

Messages

1- Ideological connection supersedes familial and kinship ties. (72) "انّه لیس من اهلك" (The reason for the salvation of a group from drowning was their faith in Noah, and nothing else).

2- Sometimes a person becomes so immersed in a good or evil deed that he seems to become the deed itself. "انّه عمل غیر صالح"

3- Prophets also need divine admonition. "انى اعظك" (Allah warns and protects His Prophets during critical moments).

4- One must avoid ignorant actions. "من الجاهلین" (Asking Allah for something that is not based on wisdom is an ignorant request).

(72) Thus, Salman becomes part of the Prophet's Household (Ahl al-Bayt), while curses are bestowed upon Abu Lahab, the Prophet's uncle.

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّىٓ أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَسْـَٔلَكَ مَا لَيْسَ لِى بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ ۖ وَإِلَّا تَغْفِرْ لِى وَتَرْحَمْنِىٓ أَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (٤۷)
He said, ‘My Lord! I seek Your protection lest I should ask You something of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy upon me I shall be among the losers.’ (47)

Messages

1- The Prophets immediately submit to Allah. «فلاتسئلن... قال ربّ انّى اعوذ بك»

2- We should not neglect the word «ربّ» (Lord) in our supplications. «ربّ انّى اعوذ...»

3- Seeking refuge (isti'adhah) and turning to Allah is the best way to insure oneself against calamities. «ربّ انّى اعوذبك»

4- We must show respect and humility in the face of Divine admonitions. «انى اعظك ... ربّ انى اعوذبك»

5- Wherever we lack knowledge of what is beneficial (maslahah), we should not make a request. «انّى اعوذ بك ان اسئلك ما لیس لى به علما»

6- Any undue question or inappropriate speech requires repentance. «والاّ تغفرلى» (If the Prophets seek refuge in Allah from undue requests, then what is the duty of us who constantly try to dictate terms to Allah?)

7- First forgiveness, and then the reception of mercy. «والاّ تغفر لى و ترحمنى»

8- The real loss is not being forgiven. «والاّ تغفر لى... اكن من الخاسرین»

9- Humans, even the Prophets, cannot reach their goal without the grace of Allah, and whoever is deprived of Divine forgiveness is a loser and a bankrupt person. «اكن من الخاسرین »

Ayah

قِيلَ يَـٰنُوحُ ٱهْبِطْ بِسَلَـٰمٍ مِّنَّا وَبَرَكَـٰتٍ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰٓ أُمَمٍ مِّمَّن مَّعَكَ ۚ وَأُمَمٌ سَنُمَتِّعُهُمْ ثُمَّ يَمَسُّهُم مِّنَّا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٤۸)
It was said, ‘O Noah! Disembark in peace from Us and with [Our] blessings upon you and upon nations [to descend] from those who are with you, and nations whom We shall provide for, then a painful punishment from Us shall befall them.’ (48)

Key Points

The command of hubūt (descent/going down) is mentioned concerning both Prophet Adam (عليه السلام) and Prophet Noah (عليه السلام), and subsequently, people were divided into two groups: believers and disbelievers. The meaning of the "nations" (umams) who were with Noah is either every single person who accompanied him, who later became the progenitor of a tribe, or it refers to each of the believers who boarded the Ark and belonged to a specific tribe.

Messages

1- Life after that widespread flood faced two major dangers: one was the contamination and muddiness of the earth, which was resolved by the word salām (peace) and the promise of safety; the other was the destruction of various types of vegetation, which was secured by the promise of blessings (barakāt). «سلام منّا وبركات علیك »

2- Although Allah sometimes preaches, warns, or even drowns a disbelieving son before his father's eyes, He also bestows His peace (salām) and blessings (barakāt) in their proper place. «سلام منّا و بركات علیك »

3- What the believer receives from divine favors is safety (salāmat) and blessing (barakah), but what the disbeliever receives from favors is merely the aspect of enjoyment (matā'). Therefore, not every enjoyment and gratification is a favor and a blessing; rather, a favor is that which is received and also has a good outcome. «سلام منّا و بركات»

4- Allah does not withhold the enjoyment (matā') of this world from the disbelievers. «سنمتّعهم ثمّ یمسّهم»

5- It is the Divine tradition that He first grants favor and respite, and then delivers the reward or the punishment. «سنمتّعهم ثمّ یمسّهم»

6- Mercy and wrath both originate from Him. «سلام منّا... یمسّهم منّا»

7- Allah informed Noah (عليه السلام) about the emergence of disbelieving societies and nations from the progeny of the believers who boarded the Ark. «و اُمم سنمتّعهم ثمّ یمسّهم منّا عذاب الیم»

Ayah

تِلْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهَآ إِلَيْكَ ۖ مَا كُنتَ تَعْلَمُهَآ أَنتَ وَلَا قَوْمُكَ مِن قَبْلِ هَـٰذَا ۖ فَٱصْبِرْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلْعَـٰقِبَةَ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤۹)
These are accounts of the Unseen, which We reveal to you. Neither you nor your people used to know them before this. So be patient. Indeed the outcome will be in favour of the Godwary. (49)

Key Points

The story of Prophet Nuh (Noah) عليه السلام is mentioned in 6 Surahs of the Holy Quran: Al-A'raf, Hud, Al-Mu'minun, Ash-Shu'ara, Al-Qamar, and Nuh. The most detailed account is found in this very Surah (Hud).

Characteristics of Nuh عليه السلام in the Quran (73): Although the name of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام is mentioned 40 times in the Quran, nothing is stated about his birthplace, residence, occupation, death, or burial place. This is because what is instructive and beneficial for us are His actions and conduct. He is the first Prophet of Ulul-'Azm (Possessors of Steadfastness) who had a universal mission, a Book, and a Sharia (Law). The prophethood of this Ulul-'Azm Prophet occurred after Prophet Adam and during the spread of polytheism, idol worship, and oppression. His program consisted of Monotheism (Tawhid), prayer (Salat), enjoining good and forbidding evil (Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy anil Munkar), justice, truthfulness, and fidelity. The duration of His mission was 950 years. When, at the end, Allah informed Him that no one else would believe in His message, He became despairing and cursed them. Consequently, even His wife and son, who were not following His path, were drowned along with the disbelievers. Prophet Nuh عليه السلام, who is known as the second father of humanity, was chosen by Allah for prophethood: «انّ اللّه اصطفى آدم و نوحاً... على العالمین» (74), and He sent greetings upon Him among the worlds: «سلام على نوح فى العالمین» (75).

The story of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام is also mentioned, with some differences, in the Torah. The books of the Chaldeans, Indians, Chinese, Greeks, and Persians have all considered the Flood of Nuh to be the wrath of God due to the oppression and corruption of the people. In the Avesta, the sacred book of the Zoroastrians, the danger of a universal flood in which all people would drown is also mentioned, stating that it was revealed to Jamshid to build a great enclosure and place all righteous men and women, and a pair of every animal, inside it.

The mission of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام was universal, because the earth cannot be without a proof (Hujjah). The drowning of all the disbelievers, based on the curse of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام when He said: «ربّ لاتذر على الارض من الكافرین دیّارا» (76), is evidence of this. Furthermore, if His mission had been regional and the flood confined to that area, there would have been no need to take aboard a pair of every animal, whereas this was done to preserve the species of animals on the earth (77).

The biography of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام is a thousand-year history that encompasses the story of a universal flood, the extinction of one generation and the beginning of a new society, a tableau of the victory of truth over falsehood, a manifestation of the Prophet's curse being answered, an example of the severing of the relationship between father and son for the sake of the faith, a sign of creation submitting to the command of Allah, the preservation of animal species, and shipbuilding far from the sea. This story shows that sometimes humans fall to such an extent that the admonition of the Prophets علیهم السلام is ineffective on them; rather, they label the Prophets' followers as rabble and seek refuge in the mountains against Divine wrath!

73) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 74) Al Imran, 33. 75) As-Saffat, 79. 76) Nuh, 26. 77) Tafsir Al-Manar claims that the mission of Prophet Nuh was regional, but Tafsir Al-Mizan strongly rejects this.

Messages

1- The Quran is the best source for becoming acquainted with human history. «تلك...»

2- Many parts of history have remained hidden from humans, one of which is the history of Prophet Nuh. «تلك من انباء الغیب»

3- The Prophet of Islam and the people had no way of knowing the history of Nuh عليه السلام except through revelation. «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك»

4- The soundest and most beneficial historical news must be received through revelation, as it comes from the source of infinite knowledge, where the transmitter is free from any accident, pressure, or negligence. «انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك»

5- The extent of the unseen knowledge (Ilm al-Ghayb) of the Prophets علیهم السلام is only that which Allah has placed at their disposal. «انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك »

6- The history and biography of previous Prophets are the best consolation for subsequent leaders. «فاصبر»

7- Patience (Sabr) and piety (Taqwa) are both necessary and conditions for victory. «فاصبر انّ العاقبة للمتقین,» instead of «فاصبر انّ العاقبة للصابرین»

8- Ultimately, the truth will be victorious and falsehood will vanish. «انّ العاقبة للمتّقین»

Ayah

وَإِلَىٰ عَادٍ أَخَاهُمْ هُودًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۖ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُفْتَرُونَ (٥٠)
And to ‘Ad [We sent] Hud, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other god besides Him: you merely fabricate [the deities that you worship]. (50)

Key Points

After Prophet Nuh (Noah), Prophet Hud was sent. The story of this Divine Prophet is also mentioned in verses 65 to 72 of Surah Al-A'raf. Since Hud عليه السلام was from the tribe of 'Ad, the verse refers to him as "اخاهم" (their brother, the brother of 'Ad). As the time of Nuh's عليه السلام passing approached, he told his companions: After me, there will be a period of occultation (Ghaybah) during which tyrants (Taghuts) will appear, and Allah will bring relief for you through a Qā'im (Riser) from my progeny whose name is Hud. (78) Taking idols as intercessors is both a slander against Allah, by considering the idols as His partners, and an accusation against the idols, by counting them as possessors of benefit and harm.

78) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Ikmal al-Din, Vol. 1, p. 135.

Messages

1- The mission of Prophet Hud was specific to the people of 'Ad. «الى عاد»

2- Propagation is more effective in a brotherly atmosphere. «اخاهم»

3- Prophets are brothers to their nations, not profiteers, nor deceivers, nor dominators and rulers. «اخاهم»

4- Invitation to Monotheism (Tawhid) is the priority of the Prophets' duties. «یا قوم اعبدوا اللّه»

5- Practical Monotheism is based on doctrinal Monotheism. «اعبدوا اللّه... ما لكم من اله غیره»

6- Associating partners with Allah (Shirk) is nothing more than imagination, illusion, and accusation. «ان انتم الا مفترون»

Ayah

يَـٰقَوْمِ لَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱلَّذِى فَطَرَنِىٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٥۱)
‘O my people! I do not ask you any reward for it. My reward lies only with Him who originated me. Do you not exercise your reason? (51)

Key Points

Since the Quran repeatedly mentions the Prophets' (علیهم السلام) refusal to seek reward and their lack of expectation from the people, it becomes clear that material affairs are among the greatest obstacles preventing people from inclining toward the Truth.

Messages

1- The Prophets do not have material goals; their objective in their work is to attract the satisfaction of Allah. «ان اجرى الاّ على الذى ...» (Allah Himself grants the Prophets their reward).

2- Sincerity (Ikhlas) is one of the essential conditions for success in propagation. «ان اجرى الاّ على الذى فطرنى»

3- Attention to the Creatorship of Allah is the foundation for sincerity. «على الذى فطرنى»

4- Reasoning guides us toward following the Prophets and adhering to revelation (Wahy). (Reason is not opposed to revelation; rather, it prepares the ground for accepting revelation.) «افلا تعقلون »

5- For someone who is not concerned with this world (Dunya) and its benefits, what reason is there for him to put himself through all this trouble?! «افلا تعقلون»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ يُرْسِلِ ٱلسَّمَآءَ عَلَيْكُم مِّدْرَارًا وَيَزِدْكُمْ قُوَّةً إِلَىٰ قُوَّتِكُمْ وَلَا تَتَوَلَّوْا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ (٥۲)
‘O my people! Plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently: He will send copious rains for you from the sky, and add power to your [present] power. So do not turn your backs [on Him] as guilty ones.’ (52)

Messages

1- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) and repentance (Tawbah) from sins is obligatory. «استغفروا... توبوا»

2- Seeking forgiveness from sins is the prelude to returning towards Allah. «استغفروا... ثم توبوا»

3- Individual seeking of forgiveness and repentance does not change the destiny of society; rather, society must transform so that Divine aid may flow down. «یا قوم استغفروا... توبوا... یرسل»

4- Deeds and beliefs are effective in natural occurrences. «توبوا... یرسل» (The people of 'Ad were afflicted by scarcity and drought due to polytheism and sin.)

5- Natural factors should not prevent us from paying attention to the Divine Will. «یرسل السماء»

6- If the Islamic system and government desires economic development, it must promote spiritual programs in society. «توبوا ...یرسل السماء»

7- The worldly reward for repentance and returning towards Allah is wealth and power. «توبوا... یرسل السماء مدرارا و یزدكم قوّة...»

8- Let us not assume that faith in Allah and attention to Him means distancing oneself from wealth and capital. «توبوا...یرسل السماء... یزدكم قوة» (If we believe, not only will nothing be demanded from our wealth, but it will also be increased for us «یزدكم »)

9- By submitting before the Divine Presence and repenting and seeking forgiveness from Him, we do not become diminished; rather, we become stronger, greater, and more honored. «یزدكم قوّة الى قوّتكم»

10- The people of 'Ad were a powerful people. «قوّة الى قوّتكم»

11- If the power of faith is added to physical strength, we become complete. «قوّة الى قوّتكم»

12- Establishing a healthy, prosperous, and powerful society is among the goals of the heavenly religions. «یا قوم...توبوا... یزدكم قوّة الى قوّتكم»

13- Economic power paves the way for other powers. First «یرسل السماء» then «یزدكم قوّة الى قوّتكم»

14- In propagation, the method of encouragement must also be used. «توبوا...یرسل... یزدكم قوّة الى قوّتكم» (Stating material results is not an obstacle on the path to achieving the goal.)

15- Turning away from the prophets and ignoring their path is a crime and a sin. «مجرمین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰهُودُ مَا جِئْتَنَا بِبَيِّنَةٍ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِتَارِكِىٓ ءَالِهَتِنَا عَن قَوْلِكَ وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (٥۳)
They said, ‘O Hud, you have not brought us any manifest proof. We are not going to abandon our gods for what you say, and we are not going to believe you. (53)

Messages

1- Those who worship stone and wood, having no rational proof for their actions, question a Prophet whose practice is the presentation of miracles and clear evidence!? «ما جئتنا ببیّنة»

2- The main assertion of the disbelievers was that they would not abandon their idols because of "your word," not due to the absence of proof. «تاركى آلهتنا عن قولك» The indication that they were not seeking proof is that they said twice: «و ما نحن بتاركى آلهتنا، و ما نحن بمؤمنین»

3- In the first stage of their invitation to the polytheists, the Prophets faced their obstinacy, but they never ceased their propagation (of the message). «ما نحن بمؤمنین »

Ayahs

إِن نَّقُولُ إِلَّا ٱعْتَرَىٰكَ بَعْضُ ءَالِهَتِنَا بِسُوٓءٍ ۗ قَالَ إِنِّىٓ أُشْهِدُ ٱللَّهَ وَٱشْهَدُوٓا۟ أَنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ (٥٤)
All we say is that some of our gods have visited you with some evil.’ He said, ‘I call Allah to witness—and you too be [my] witnesses—that I repudiate what you take as [His] partners (54)
مِن دُونِهِۦ ۖ فَكِيدُونِى جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ لَا تُنظِرُونِ (٥٥)
besides Him. Now try out your stratagems against me, together, without granting me any respite. (55)

Key Points

"I'tirā'" (اعتراء) means to object (or attack) and to strike (or afflict). Prophet Hūd, in order to prove the futility of the idolaters' imaginary powers, challenged them to a confrontation, just as Prophet Nūḥ عليه السلام said to his people: «فاجمعوا امركم و شركائكم ثمّ لایكن امركم علیكم غمّة، ثم اقضوا الىّ و لاتنظرون»(79) Do whatever you wish to do, but know that no harm will reach me. The Prophet of Islam also said: «قل ادعوا شركائكم ثم كیدون فلاتنظرون»(80) Call upon your partners (in worship), then scheme against me and grant me no respite.

79) Yūnus, 71. 80) Al-A'rāf, 195.

Messages

1- The people of 'Ād believed that each of their idols and deities was effective in a specific aspect of the world's affairs. «بعض آلهتنا»

2- Accusing the Prophets, tradition-breakers, and social reformers who rise up against superstitions of madness is not a new phenomenon. «اعتراك ...بسوء»

3- Declaring dissociation and aversion from polytheism (shirk) and polytheists has been the practice and method of the Divine Prophets. «برئ ممّا تشركون »

4- One must stand decisively against superstitions. «انّى برىٌ مما تشركون»

5- The challenge issued by Hūd عليه السلام is proof of his righteousness, decisiveness, and the truth of his path. «فكیدونى...»

6- The Prophets fear no power. «فكیدونى جمیعاً»

7- Idols possess no power whatsoever. «فكیدونى جمیعاً»

Ayah

إِنِّى تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ رَبِّى وَرَبِّكُم ۚ مَّا مِن دَآبَّةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ ءَاخِذٌۢ بِنَاصِيَتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٥٦)
Indeed I have put my trust in Allah, my Lord and your Lord. There is no living being but He holds it by its forelock. Indeed my Lord is on a straight path. (56)

Messages

1- Attention to the comprehensive Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah is the basis for reliance (Tawakkul) upon Him. «توكّلت... ربّى وربّكم»

2- Reliance (Tawakkul) is the basis for courage. «فكیدونى جمیعاً... انى توكّلت» (By relying and depending upon Allah, one can stand against the whole world.)

3- No creature (dābbah) is capable of harming another without the Will of Allah. «فكیدونى جمیعاً... ما من دابّة الاّ و هو آخذ بناصیتها»

4- Allah has complete dominance over everything and is also just. «آخذ بناصیتها، على صراط مستقیم»

5- We should rely upon someone who possesses justice in addition to power. «توكّلت , آخذ , على صراط مستقیم»

6- In the face of the obstinacy and stubbornness of the disbelievers (kuffār), one must speak of Divine wrath and justice. «آخذ بناصیتها، على صراط مستقیم »

Ayah

فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُم مَّآ أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَيَسْتَخْلِفُ رَبِّى قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ وَلَا تَضُرُّونَهُۥ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ حَفِيظٌ (٥۷)
But if you turn your backs [on me], then [know that] I have communicated to you whatever I was sent to you with. My Lord will make another people succeed you, and you will not hurt Allah in the least. Indeed my Lord is watchful over all things.’ (57)

Messages

1- The Prophets establish the conclusive proof after inviting and conveying the Divine command. «فان تولّوا فقد ابلغتكم»

2- The responsibility of the preacher is the explanation of religious knowledge, not compulsion regarding it. «فان تولّوا فقد ابلغتكم»

3- The preacher of religion and the leader of the community should not consider the people's turning away a sign of His own weakness or failure. «فان تولوا فقد ابلغتكم »

4- The establishment or extinction of communities is not without calculation or accidental; rather, it follows laws and traditions that Allah has decreed. «یستخلف ربى قوماً غیركم» (Shirk [polytheism], sin, and opposition to the Prophets are among the factors leading to the destruction of societies and historical transformations.)

5- Our disbelief, disobedience, death, and destruction do not cause any harm to Allah. «ان تولّوا، یستخلف... غیركم و لا تضرّونه»

6- Allah is the Guardian (Hafiz) over everything and everyone; therefore, our disbelief and conspiracy do not harm Him. «انّ ربّى على كلّ شى حفیظ »

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا هُودًا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ (٥۸)
When Our edict came, We delivered Hud and the faithful who were with him, by a mercy from Us, and We delivered them from a harsh punishment. (58)

Messages

1- Just as the descent of punishment is by the command of Allah, rescue from it is also by His will. «نجّینا»

2- Prophets, their companions, and their followers are immune from Divine wrath. «نجّینا هوداً و الذین آمنوا معه»

3- Mere faith in the Prophets is not sufficient or effective; rather, being with them and supporting them is also necessary. «معه»

4- The source of mercy is Him, «رحمة منّا», but the source of His wrath and anger is our own action. « عذاب غلیظ» and He did not say: «عذاب منّا»

5- The repetition of the word for salvation is perhaps a symbol of the freedom of the Prophet's followers from Allah's wrath in this world and the Hereafter. «نجّینا... نجّینا»

6- Winds are also Divine agents. «امرنا» (81) 81) The Divine command is what we read in Surah Fussilat, Ayah 15: «فارسلنا علیهم ریحاً صرصراً فى ایّام نحسات» We sent a strong wind upon them during ominous days.

Ayah

وَتِلْكَ عَادٌ ۖ جَحَدُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَعَصَوْا۟ رُسُلَهُۥ وَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (٥۹)
Such were [the people of] Ad: they impugned the signs of their Lord and disobeyed His apostles, and followed the dictates of every obstinate tyrant. (59)

Messages

1- The history of the predecessors is before you; why do you not take a lesson? «تلك»

2- Denying one Prophet is equivalent to denying all Prophets.(82) «عصوا رسله»

3- The dangers that threaten a society are:

A: Disbelief in the divine signs (Ayat). «جحدوا بایات ربّهم »

B: Disobedience to the Prophets and the true leaders. «عصوا رسله »

C: Following the tyrants (Taghuts). «اتبعوا امر كلّ جبّار»

4- Whoever is not a follower of the infallible Prophets علیهم السلام will become a follower of stubborn oppressors. «عصوا رسله ...اتبعوا امر كلّ جبار»

5- The compulsion (or tyranny) of the oppressors (jabbars) is due to the obedience of the masses. «اتّبعوا... جبّار»

82) The people of 'Ad apparently had only one Prophet, and that was Hadrat Hud. Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

وَأُتْبِعُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعْنَةً وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ عَادًا كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّعَادٍ قَوْمِ هُودٍ (٦٠)
So they were pursued by a curse in this world and on the Day of Resurrection. Behold! ‘Ad indeed defied their Lord. Now, away with ‘Ad, the people of Hud! (60)

Key Points

From Ayah 50 to Ayah 60 of this Surah, the text relates to the people of 'Ad. These people were of Arab descent and resided in the Arabian Peninsula (Jazirat al-Arab), and their name is not found in the existing Torah. According to verses from the blessed Surahs of Al-Qamar, Al-Haqqah, Al-A'raf, As-Sajdah, and Ash-Shu'ara, they were a strong, tall people, possessing prosperous cities and an unparalleled civilization. "اِرمَ ذات العماد التى لم یخلق مثلها فى البلاد" Ideologically, they were idolaters and followers of the Taghut (tyrant/false deity). Prophet Hud عليه السلام invited them to monotheism (Tawhid) and the worship of Allah, but they refused and were destroyed by the wrath of Allah through a fierce and scorching wind. The people of 'Ad consisted of two groups: 'Ad the First and 'Ad the Second. 'Ad the Second lived seven hundred years before Prophet Christ عليه السلام in Al-Ahqaf (a region in Hijaz) or Yemen.(83)

83) Tafsir al-Mizan and Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Whoever denies the divine verses, disobeys the Prophets of Allah, and follows the Taghut is far from divine mercy. "و اُتبعوا... لعنة"

2- Ill repute in this world is a divine punishment. "و اتبعوا فى هذه الدنیا لعنة"

3- The slogan, "Death to the oppressor," is a Quranic slogan. "الا بُعداً لعاد"(84) 84) "الا بُعداً لثمود" (Hud, 68), "الا بُعداً لِمَدین" (Hud, 95), " فبُعداً للقوم الظالمین" (Al-Mu'minun, 41) and "فبُعداً لقوم لایؤمنون" (Al-Mu'minun, 44).

Ayah

۞ وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌ مُّجِيبٌ (٦۱)
And to Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other god besides Him. He brought you forth from the earth and made it your habitation. So plead with Him for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. My Lord is indeed nearmost [and] responsive.’ (61)

Key Points

| Prophet Salih عليه السلام is the third Prophet who was sent to prophethood after Prophets Nuh and Hud عليهما السلام.

Messages

1- The relationship between the Prophets and the people was a fraternal relationship. «اخاهم»

2- It is better for the preacher to be from the inhabitants of the region being preached to. «اخاهم»

3- The goal of all Prophets is the worship of the One God. «اعبدوا اللّه، مالكم من اله غیره»

4- The reason and philosophy behind worshipping Allah is His boundless grace toward humanity. «اعبدوا، انشأكم»

5- The origin of human creation is from the earth (dust). «انشأكم من الارض»

6- Islam is a comprehensive religion encompassing both this world and the Hereafter (both developing the earth and seeking forgiveness and repentance). «واستعمركم فیها فاستغفروه»

7- Allah's desire is the development and prosperity of the earth. «واستعمركم فیها»

8- The Worshipped One of the monotheists possesses understanding, consciousness, knowledge, power, hearing, and responsiveness to them, qualities which idols lack. «قریب مجیب»

9- Returning to Allah is easy, and repentance is the means of drawing near to Him. «توبوا الیه انّ ربّى قریب مجیب»

10- If there is seeking forgiveness and repentance, Allah accepts it. «توبوا... قریب مجیب»

11- Allah is near to everyone; it is we, our thoughts, and our actions that cause our distance from Him. «ربّى قریب مجیب»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰصَـٰلِحُ قَدْ كُنتَ فِينَا مَرْجُوًّا قَبْلَ هَـٰذَآ ۖ أَتَنْهَىٰنَآ أَن نَّعْبُدَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا وَإِنَّنَا لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَآ إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ (٦۲)
They said, ‘O Salih! You were a source of hope to us before this. Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers have been worshiping? Indeed we have grave doubts concerning that to which you invite us.’ (62)

Key Points

The disbelievers initially said to Prophet Salih (عليه السلام): You were a source of hope for us, and we loved you, hoping that by this means, he would abandon calling them to monotheism in order to preserve the good relations of the past.

Messages

1- The preacher must have a good reputation and a good history. marjūwan

2- The praise and commendation of the misguided should not weaken us in calling others to the truth. qad kunta fīnā marjūwan (The flattery and praise of enemies are not without motive.)

3- Prophets are the pioneers who break through superstitious beliefs. a-tanhanā

4- Human beliefs should not be based solely on the practices of ancestors and predecessors. na‘budu mā ya‘budu ābā’unā

5- Human cultures have become interconnected and intertwined over long centuries. na‘budu mā ya‘budu ābā’unā

6- People do not easily abandon the beliefs and convictions of their ancestors. (One should not expect immediate reform.) innana lafī shakk

7- Every new statement or plan is suspicious and doubtful for some people. innana lafī shakk

8- If doubt is not a prelude and basis for research and guidance, it will become the greatest factor leading to stagnation and downfall. shakk...murīb

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَءَاتَىٰنِى مِنْهُ رَحْمَةً فَمَن يَنصُرُنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِنْ عَصَيْتُهُۥ ۖ فَمَا تَزِيدُونَنِى غَيْرَ تَخْسِيرٍ (٦۳)
He said, ‘O my people! Tell me, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has granted me His own mercy, who will protect me from Allah should I disobey Him? For then you will increase me in nothing but loss. (63)

Messages

1- Heavenly religions and divine leaders invite people to Allah and Monotheism, not through force, threat, or ignorance, but through clear proof (Bayyinah), miracles, and bright evidences. «بیّنة م ن ربّى»

2- The station of prophethood is a special divine mercy upon specific servants. «وآتانى منه رحمةً»

3- Prophets, in case of negligence in their mission, will be afflicted by divine wrath. «فمن ینصرنى من اللَّه ان عصیته»

4- We should not abandon Allah and His path based on the pleasant thought of people's approval and their support, because deviated people add nothing but loss to others. «فماتزیدوننى غیر تخسیر»

5- Opposition to the truth is loss. «تخسیر»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةً فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ قَرِيبٌ (٦٤)
O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Let her graze [freely] in Allah’s land, and do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by a prompt punishment.’ (64)

Key Points

The she-camel of Prophet Salih was exceptional in many ways: (85) 1- It emerged from the heart of the mountain. 2- It was pregnant without contact with a male camel. 3- It drank all the water of the village for one day. 4- It gave milk sufficient for the needs of the people of the region. 5- The people had no responsibility towards it. Prophet Salih عليه السلام said to the people: I will request something from your idols, and you should also request something from my God. Whichever—the idols or God—gives a positive response, we shall all worship Him. The polytheists accepted. Their request was that right now, a female camel, while also being pregnant, should emerge from the heart of this mountain. Allah granted their request, and the camel emerged from the mountain with all these conditions. (86)

85) The story of Salih's she-camel is also mentioned in verses of Surah Ash-Shu'ara (155 - 158) and Surah Al-Qamar (27 - 31). 86) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The proof and miracle of the Divine Prophet must be tangible, understandable, and visible to the people. «هذه»

2- The Hand of Allah's power is open, and He is the Creator of means; He can make the emergence of a camel from the heart of a mountain the miracle of a prophet. «هذه ناقة اللَّه»

3- Salih's she-camel was a great miracle. «ناقة اللَّه لكم آیة»

4- Sanctities must be respected. «فذروها , لاتمسّوها»

5- Insulting sanctities leads to immediate punishment from Allah. «عذاب قریب»

Ayah

فَعَقَرُوهَا فَقَالَ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ فِى دَارِكُمْ ثَلَـٰثَةَ أَيَّامٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ وَعْدٌ غَيْرُ مَكْذُوبٍ (٦٥)
But they hamstrung her, whereupon he said, ‘Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days: that is a promise not to be belied!’ (65)

Key Points

We read in narrations that the killer of the she-camel was only one person, yet the Quran says: «عقروها» (they hamstrung her), meaning all the people killed her. This was due to the consensus and satisfaction of the others with the killer's action, because, from the perspective of Islam, the ideological connection between individuals causes the sin of any one person to be counted against his supporters and backers as well. (87)

Question: What is the philosophy behind the three-day respite?

Answer: A: An opportunity for repentance. B: A leverage point for psychological pressure and doubling the torment. If a person is told that he will be destroyed in 3 days, this news itself inflicts the greatest blow and pressure on his soul and psyche. C: Another proof of the divine Prophet's truthfulness, because setting a specific time is itself a form of knowledge of the unseen (al-ghayb).

87) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, pp. 409 - 411.

Messages

1- Being satisfied with the sins of others means becoming their partner in crime. (88) «فعقروها»

2- Do not consider divine warnings to be a joke or a lie. «غیر مكذوب»

3- Insulting sacred things results in certain punishment. «وعد غیر مكذوب»

88) This meaning is also referred to in Nahj al-Balāghah, in Sayings 12 and 20.

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا صَـٰلِحًا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَمِنْ خِزْىِ يَوْمِئِذٍ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ (٦٦)
So when Our edict came, We delivered Salih and the faithful who were with him by a mercy from Us and from the [punishment and] disgrace of that day. Your Lord is indeed the All-strong, the All-mighty. (66)

Key Points

The word khizy (خِزى) means a defect (or flaw) whose manifestation causes disgrace and dishonor for a person. Natural calamities such as earthquakes, floods, or dangerous diseases may afflict both the believing and the disbelieving groups, similar to the consequences of carelessness that originate from humans and whose effects spread to everyone. However, where Divine wrath is involved, the believers will be safe, unless they too have deserved punishment due to silence in the face of oppression or abandoning the forbidding of evil (Nahy an al-Munkar). « نجّینا صالحاً و الذین امنوا معه»

Messages

1- Allah protects the Prophets and their companions from His wrath and punishment. «نجّینا»

2- The condition for salvation from Allah's wrath is faith and following the Prophet. «نجّینا... الذین آمنوامعه»

3- Following the Prophet is the basis for human honor and dignity. «نجّینا... الذین آمنوا... من خزى یومئذ»

4- Saving a few believers from among so many disbelievers is not difficult for Allah. «القوىّ العزیز»

5- Allah consoles the Prophet of Islam that He can deal with his opponents in the same manner, because He is the Powerful, the Mighty. «ان ربّك هو القوى العزیز»

Ayahs

وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (٦۷)
The Cry seized those who were wrongdoers, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes, (67)
كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ثَمُودَا۟ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّثَمُودَ (٦۸)
as if they had never lived there. Behold! Thamud indeed defied their Lord. Now, away with Thamud! (68)

Key Points

"Jāthimīn" (جاثِمین), derived from "Jathm" (جَثْم), means sitting on the knees or falling onto the face, a state like being electrocuted, which freezes a person in whatever condition they are in and takes away their power to flee. The word "Yaghnaw" (یَغنوا) is derived from the root "Ghinā" (غَنى), meaning to reside in a place. The file (or case) of the stubborn and oppressive people of Thamūd is closed in this Ayah. Prophet Salih عليه السلام was one of the Arab prophets sent to the people of Thamūd, who lived in Wādī al-Qurā (a land between Medina and Sham/Syria), and their occupation was agriculture and gardening. The name of Prophet Salih عليه السلام is not found in the current Torah.(89) Just as today, when supersonic airplanes break the sound barrier, the resulting tremendous noise shatters glass, causes pregnant women to miscarry, and accelerates heartbeats, the end of the world's lifespan will also be accompanied by a blast (ṣayḥah) through which everything will collapse. «ما ینظرون الاّ صیحة واحدة تأخذهم و هم یخصّمون»(90) Similarly, the Resurrection (Qiyāmah) and the scene of the Gathering (Maḥshar) will also come into existence through a blast. «ان كانت الاّ صیحة واحدة، فاذا هم جمیعٌ لدینا محضرون»(91)

89) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 90) Yā Sīn, 49. 91) Yā Sīn, 53.

Messages

1- The descent of divine wrath and punishment upon the oppressors is the result of their own injustice and oppression. «اخذ الذین ظلموا»

2- Divine retribution is not limited to the Day of Judgment; oppression and injustice are also punished in this world. «اخذ الذین ظلموا»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ بِٱلْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًا ۖ قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌ ۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَن جَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ حَنِيذٍ (٦۹)
Certainly Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news, and said, ‘Peace!’ ‘Peace!’ He replied. Presently he brought [for them] a roasted calf. (69)

Messages

1- Angels, by divine permission, take the form and shape of humans. «جاءت رسلناابراهیم»

2- Speech should be initiated with a greeting (Salam). «قالوا سلاماً»

3- Salam is a heavenly slogan and a celestial method. «قالوا سلاماً»

4- The greeting (Salam) must be answered in a better manner. (The nominal sentence «سلامٌ» indicates greater permanence and certainty than the verbal sentence «قالوا سلاماً».)

5- One must hasten in hospitality. «فما لبث»

6- Hosting a guest is a virtue, even if the guest is unknown. «جاء بعجل حنیذٍ»

7- Abraham علیه السلام was hospitable and generous. «جاء بعجل حنیذٍ»

8- We should not question the guest about food. (Do you want food? Have you eaten? What food do you want?) «جاء بعِجل»

9- In hospitality, we should serve directly ourselves. «جاء بعِجل»

10- We should bring the food to the guest, not the guest to the food. «جاء بعجل»

11- Hospitality toward the guest should be desirable and special.(92) «بعجل حنیذ» (92) We read in Surah Adh-Dhariyat that the hospitality of Prophet Abraham علیه السلام toward his guests involved roasting a fat calf. «عجل سمین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَءَآ أَيْدِيَهُمْ لَا تَصِلُ إِلَيْهِ نَكِرَهُمْ وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ۚ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (۷٠)
But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he took them amiss and felt a fear of them. They said, ‘Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot.’ (70)

Key Points

The feeling of danger experienced by Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) is different from the fear and weakness that people usually suffer when encountering issues, because he was the idol-breaker of history and never feared anything. However, being aware of danger and malicious intent is another matter. Hierarchy must be observed: Since Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) and his people were one of the branches under the command of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام), Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) was informed first regarding the destruction of the people of Lot.

Messages

1- Angels do not consume material food. «لا تصل الیه»

2- The knowledge of the Prophets is limited. «نكرهم»

3- In the era of Abraham (عليه السلام), a guest refusing to eat was a sign of hostility toward the host. «اوجس منهم خیفة»

4- Sometimes the root of human fear is ignorance. «اوجس منهم... لاتخف انّا اُرسلنا»

5- One of the missions of the angels is to bring punishment. «اُرسلنا الى قوم لوط»

Ayah

وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ قَآئِمَةٌ فَضَحِكَتْ فَبَشَّرْنَـٰهَا بِإِسْحَـٰقَ وَمِن وَرَآءِ إِسْحَـٰقَ يَعْقُوبَ (۷۱)
His wife, standing by, laughed as We gave her the good news of [the birth of] Isaac, and of Jacob, after Isaac. (71)

Key Points

The word ḍiḥk (ضِحك) means laughing, but the word ḍaḥk (ضَحك) means menstruation (the monthly habit). The laughter of the wife of Abraham (عليه السلام) was either because she realized that the guests not eating was not a sign of danger for Abraham, and thus she became happy. Or, as mentioned in some tafsirs (commentaries), Sarah, the wife of Abraham (عليه السلام), was an elderly woman who had not menstruated for a long time, but immediately upon hearing the news, this state suddenly came upon her, and she understood that the Divine Will had decreed that she would bear a child. Just as Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: The meaning of ḍaḥakat (ضَحكت) is her menstruation. (93)

93) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Sometimes the presence of a woman on the scene is necessary. «وأمرأته قائمة»

2- The spirit/temperament of women is more delicate than that of men. (Both Abraham (عليه السلام) and his wife Sarah heard the incident, but only Sarah laughed.) «فضحكت»

3- Amidst anxieties, there is also good news (glad tidings). «اوجس منهم خیفة... فبشّرناها»

4- Among the best glad tidings is a continuous and lasting blessing. «و من وراء اسحاق یعقوب» (94)

5- The names of the Divine Prophets are predetermined. «اسحاق , یعقوب»

6- Although every group of Divine angels has a specific mission, this group of messengers pursued several objectives: one was the eradication and destruction of the corrupt people of Lot, and the other was the glad tidings of a child to Abraham (عليه السلام) and Sarah. «ارسلنا الى قوم لوط، فبشّرناها باسحاق»

94) Perhaps the naming of Jacob (Ya’qub), which comes from the word ‘aqib (meaning 'following' or 'heel'), is because he comes after Isaac (Isḥāq). Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

قَالَتْ يَـٰوَيْلَتَىٰٓ ءَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا۠ عَجُوزٌ وَهَـٰذَا بَعْلِى شَيْخًا ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ عَجِيبٌ (۷۲)
She said, ‘Oh, my! Shall I, an old woman, bear [children], and [while] this husband of mine is an old man?! That is indeed an odd thing!’ (72)

Key Points

The wife of Ibrāhīm (Abraham) عليه السلام truly had the right to be astonished, because she was an 'ajūzun 'aqīm (barren old woman) (95). Meaning, besides being old and having an age of about ninety years, she was also infertile in her youth, and Ibrāhīm عليه السلام was also one hundred years old. According to Tafsir al-Mīzān, ba'l (بَعْل) is used for someone who is not dependent on others and can stand on his own feet. Since a husband can provide for his expenses independently, he is called ba'l. The term ba'l is also applied to a palm tree that does not require irrigation.

(95) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 29.

Messages

1- A woman can reach a stage where she speaks with angels. "قالت"

2- Expressing astonishment at the manifestation of Divine power does not contradict faith in Allah. "انّ هذا لشى عجیب"

3- We should not confine the power of Allah within our limited capabilities. "أنا عجوز، بعلى شیخاً" (Apparent means and causes are not always effective.)

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَعْجَبِينَ مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ رَحْمَتُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَـٰتُهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَمِيدٌ مَّجِيدٌ (۷۳)
They said, ‘Are you amazed at Allah’s dispensation? [That is] Allah’s mercy and His blessings upon you, members of the household. Indeed He is all-laudable, all-glorious.’ (73)

Key Points

One day, Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام greeted a group. They replied: «عليک السلام و رحمة اللّه و بركاته عليكم اهل البيت و مغفرته و رضوانه» (Peace be upon you, and the Mercy of Allah and His Blessings upon you, O People of the House, and His Forgiveness and His Pleasure). The Imam said: In response to a greeting, do not add more than what the angels said to Ibrahim عليه السلام. Therefore, the phrase «و رحمة اللّه و بركاته» is sufficient. (96)

Question: Considering that in the above Ayah, the angels addressed the wife of Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام as Ahl al-Bayt (People of the House), and naturally, the wife of any individual is considered the people of his house, why are the wives of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله not counted among His Ahl al-Bayt in the Ayah of Purification: «انّما یرید اللّه لیذهب عنكم الرجس اهل البیت و یطهركم تطهیرا» (97)?

Answer: If we rely solely on the literal meaning of the word, the term Ahl al-Bayt naturally applies to a person's wife. However, sometimes we have evidence that excludes an individual from this meaning, similar to the Ayah «انّه لیس مِن اهلك» (98), which does not count the son of Nuh عليه السلام among his family. Or sometimes evidence is found that includes an individual in this matter, such as what is narrated concerning Salman, where the Holy Prophet said: «سلمان منّا اهل البیت» (Salman is from us, the People of the House) (99). Regarding the Ayah of Purification, there are also numerous narrations indicating that the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, by taking specific individuals under his cloak (aba), named only them as His Ahl al-Bayt and did not permit even His wife, Umm Salamah, to enter.

96) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 97) Al-Ahzab, 33. 98) Hud, 46. 99) Bihar, Vol. 18, p. 19.

Messages

1- Sometimes angels speak and converse with people other than the Prophets. «قالوا أتعجبين»

2- A woman can also be addressed by celestial beings. «قالوا أتعجبين» (The angels forbade the wife of Prophet Ibrahim from incredulity.)

3- Never despair of divine unseen assistance from Allah. «أتعجبين من امر اللّه»

4- To remove incredulity and astonishment regarding a matter, we should recall the countless blessings of Allah. «أتعجبين... رحمت اللَّه و بركاته عليكم» (The same God Who made the fire cool for Ibrahim عليه السلام and granted him victory over the idolaters can bestow children upon a barren old woman and an aged husband.)

5- A righteous child is mercy and blessing from Allah. «رحمت اللَّه و بركاته عليكم»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنْ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلرَّوْعُ وَجَآءَتْهُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ يُجَـٰدِلُنَا فِى قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (۷٤)
So when the awe had left Abraham and the good news had reached him, he pleaded with Us concerning the people of Lot. (74)

Key Points

Perhaps the argument (mujādalah) of Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام is the same as what is mentioned in Surah Al-Ankabut, Ayah 31, where he said to the angels: How can you overturn the region while Prophet Lot عليه السلام, the Messenger of Allah, is among them? They replied: We have complete knowledge of their condition, and we will save Lot and his companions.

Messages

1- Knowledge is the prerequisite for tranquility. (Because Ibrahim عليه السلام became aware, he became calm.) «ذهب عن ابراهیم الروع»

2- Fear in the Prophets is accidental (temporary), not inherent or intrinsic. «ذهب»

3- First repel harm, then attract benefit. «ذهب عن ابراهیم الروع و جائته البشرى»

4- Spiritual tranquility is the basis for paying attention to one's fellow human beings. «فلما ذهب عن ابراهیم الروع»

5- We should not take a stance during moments of agitation. «ذهب عن ابراهیم الروع... یجادلنا»

6- Glad tidings of a blessing should not make us oblivious to the fate of others. «جائته البشرى یجادلنا فى قوم لوط»

7- The Prophets are concerned about the people under their care. «یجادلنا فى قوم لوط»

8- We should not only be concerned about our own tribe and kin. «یجادلنا فى قوم لوط»

9- In cases where Divine decrees are not absolute, insistence, intercession (shafā'ah), supplication (du'ā), and seeking means (tawassul) are effective. «یجادلنا»

10- Arguing (jidāl) with the angels and the Divine agents is arguing with Allah. «یجادلنا»

Ayahs

إِنَّ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّٰهٌ مُّنِيبٌ (۷٥)
Abraham was indeed most forbearing, plaintive, [and] penitent. (75)
يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَـٰذَآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ قَدْ جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَإِنَّهُمْ ءَاتِيهِمْ عَذَابٌ غَيْرُ مَرْدُودٍ (۷٦)
‘O Abraham, let this matter alone! Your Lord’s edict has certainly come, and an irrevocable punishment shall overtake them.’ (76)

Key Points

In Ayah 74, Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام argued with the angels concerning the punishment of the people of Lot. In Ayah 76, [Allah] says: O Ibrahim! Desist from this argument and insistence. Between these two Ayahs, which refer to Ibrahim's عليه السلام argument and Allah's reminder to him, Ayah 75 states, in order to preserve his character and status, and to show that his argument was based solely on compassion for the nation, and not denial or anything else, that Ibrahim عليه السلام is halīm (forbearing), awwāh (oft-turning/lamenting), and munīb (penitent/returning).

Messages

1- Prophets are the most compassionate leaders of human society, and they strive for the salvation of people through forbearance, lamentation, and turning [to God]. «حلیم اوّاه منیب»

2- Reminders must be accompanied by the preservation of the status and character of individuals. «ان ابراهیم لحلیم اوّاه منیب»

3- Ibrahim's intercession for the people occurred before he was informed that the punishment had become definite. «قد جاء امر ربّك»

4- Divine punishment is also part of the path of human training/nurturing. «امر ربّك»

5- Sometimes, even the intercession of the Prophets is not accepted. When the definite command of Allah is issued, no authority can prevent it. «یا ابراهیم اعرض عن هذا»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًا سِىٓءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا وَقَالَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌ (۷۷)
When Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account and in a predicament for their sake, and he said, ‘This is a terrible day!’ (77)

Key Points

Since the divine messengers came to Prophet Lot in the form of handsome youths and became his guests, he became worried about the evil intentions of his corrupt people towards his guests. The phrase «ضاق بهم ذرعا» means "his hand was shortened," which is a metaphor for him becoming helpless and having his way blocked.

Messages

1- The knowledge of the prophets is limited. «لمّا جائت رسلنا» (Prophet Lot did not know that these guests were angels in human form.)

2- The first stage of combating evil (munkar) is the stage of the heart. « سِى ءَ بهم»

3- Protecting the guest is the responsibility of the host. «ضاق بهم ذرعا»

Ayah

وَجَآءَهُۥ قَوْمُهُۥ يُهْرَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ وَمِن قَبْلُ كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِى هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ فِى ضَيْفِىٓ ۖ أَلَيْسَ مِنكُمْ رَجُلٌ رَّشِيدٌ (۷۸)
Then his people came running toward him, and they had been committing vices aforetime. He said, ‘O my people, these are my daughters: they are purer for you. Be wary of Allah and do not humiliate me with regard to my guests. Is there not a right-minded man among you?’ (78)

Key Points

«Yuhra'ūn» (یُهرَعون) derives from «ihrā'» (اِهراع), meaning severe driving or rushing. This means that the unrestrained instinct of lust was driving this rebellious people toward the guests of Prophet Lot عليه السلام. The intent of «aṭhar» (اَطهر - purer) is not that sodomy is pure and marriage is purer (100), but rather it is a form of leniency (or accommodation) toward the sinner, implying that if you seek to satisfy lust, marriage is better than sin (101). It is amidst the twists and turns of anxiety, apprehension, and difficulties that talents and spiritual states blossom. If the divine angels had introduced themselves to Prophet Lot عليه السلام from the moment they arrived, there would have been no lamentation, worry, appeal to the people's sentiments, or proposal of marriage. Question: How could a Prophet marry his believing daughter to a disbelieving man? Answer: Perhaps they stipulated faith at the time of marriage so that this connection and marriage would become a means for their faith, or perhaps the intent of «banātī» (بناتى - my daughters) refers to the daughters of the people, because a Prophet is like a father to the community. It is also possible that such a union was permissible at that time.

100) Similar to «khayr» (خير - better) in «Qul mā ‘indallāhi khayrun min al-lahw» (Say, "What is with Allah is better than diversion") (Surah Al-Jumu'ah, 11). 101) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Sometimes human society falls so low that people rush toward deviation and sin. «Yuhra'ūn ilayh» (یُهرعون الیه)

2- The rebellious soul drives man toward sin. «Yuhra'ūn» (یُهرعون)

3- By committing any sin, the ground is prepared for subsequent sins. «Wa min qablu kānū ya‘malūna al-sayyi’āt» (و من قبل كانوا یعملون السیئات)

4- To prevent evil deeds, one must first open and show the people the paths of righteousness (the known good). «Hā’ulā’i banātī, lā takhzūn» (هؤلاء بناتى , لاتخزون)

5- The meaning of chastity and purity is not isolation, but rather observing purity in natural and ordinary interaction. «Hā’ulā’i banātī» (هؤلاء بناتى) (Prophet Lot points to His daughters in the presence of the men).

6- For the guidance of society, mere preaching is not enough; in some cases, sacrifice must be made. «Banātī» (بناتى)

7- There is no impediment to the proposal of marriage coming from the father of the daughter. «Hā’ulā’i banātī» (هؤلاء بناتى)

8- Marriage is the only correct and pure way to satisfy the sexual instinct, and other ways are wrong and impure. «Hunna aṭharu lakum» (هنّ اطهرلكم)

9- The normalization of sin does not negate a person's duty of Nahy ‘anil-Munkar (enjoining the right and forbidding the wrong). «Wa min qablu kānū ya‘malūna al-sayyi’āt, fa-ittaqū Allāh» (و من قبل كانوا یعملون السیئات , فاتقوا الله)

10- Homosexuality was forbidden, detested, and rejected in past religions as well. «Fa-ittaqū Allāh» (فاتّقوا اللَّه)

11- Even though the probability of effect is very low, one must still forbid the wrong. «Fa-ittaqū Allāh» (فاتّقوااللَّه)

12- Insulting and harming a guest is considered an insult and harm to the host. «Lā takhzūnī fī ḍayfī» (لا تخزون فى ضیفى)

13- The value of hospitality is such that the noblest of people endure the greatest suffering for its sake. «Lā takhzūnī fī ḍayfī» (لا تخزون فى ضیفى)

14- Defending a guest is a human right, and indifference toward him is an ignoble act. «Lā takhzūnī fī ḍayfī» (لا تخزون فى ضیفى)

15- In forbidding the wrong, we should seek help from people's emotions and sentiments. «A laysa minkum rajulun rashīd» (ألیس منكم رجل رشید)

16- Homosexuality is incompatible with manliness, valor, and honor (ghayrah). «A laysa minkum rajulun rashīd» (ألیس منكم رجل رشید)

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا لَنَا فِى بَنَاتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍّ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ (۷۹)
They said, ‘You certainly know that we have no interest in your daughters, and indeed you know what we want.’ (79)
قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِى بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ ءَاوِىٓ إِلَىٰ رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ (۸٠)
He said, ‘If only I had the power to deter you, or could take refuge in a mighty support!’ (80)

Key Points

Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) says in this Ayah: If I had believing supporters, I would surely rise up to fight you vile people and defend my guests, or at least I would take them to a safe place and give them refuge. The Noble Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله), while reciting this Ayah, said: «رحمةاللَّه على لوط، لقد كان یاوى الى ركن شدید» (102) (Mercy of Allah be upon Lot, he surely sought refuge in a strong support). It seems that the intent of the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) is that [Lot] passed away, joined God, and was relieved from the evil of his people.

Homosexuality (sodomy) is one of the major sins and carries the prescribed Hadd (punishment) of execution and lashing, which is executable upon the testimony of just individuals or the confession of the perpetrators.

The Ugliness of the Sin of Liwat (Sodomy) in the Quran and Hadith

The Glorious Quran, in various Ayahs that narrate the story of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) and his people, uses different terms for the ugly and detested act of this deviated people, which indicates the magnitude of the sin and the severity of its prohibition:

1- Fahsha (Abomination). «اتأتون الفاحشة» (103) 2- Israf (Extravagance/Transgression). «بل انتم قوم مسرفون» (104) 3- Jurm (Crime). «كیف كان عاقبة المجرمین» (105) 4- Dhulm (Injustice/Wrongdoing). «و ماهى من الظالمین ببعید» (106) 5 - Khabath (Wickedness). «كانت تعمل الخبائث» (107) 6- Jahalat (Ignorance). «بل انتم قوم تجهلون» (108) 7- Fisq (Transgression/Defiance). «انهم كانوا قوم سوء فاسقین» (109)

Severe threats are also seen in narrations regarding this matter, including that whenever Liwat is committed with a youth, the Divine Throne trembles, and on the Day of Judgment, Allah will not speak to them. (110)

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: If it were decreed that someone should be stoned twice, that person would be the one who practices Liwat. (111)

The Noble Messenger (صلى الله علیه وآله) also said: Whoever insists on committing Liwat with others will not die until he invites people to commit Liwat with him. (112)

In the act of Liwat, both the active and passive participants are executed, and the reason for its prohibition in narrations is identified as corruption (fasad) and the cutting off of progeny (113).

In numerous narrations, women who make themselves resemble men and men who make themselves resemble women, thereby setting the stage for the ugly act of Liwat, have been severely condemned.
