Messages

1- Due to sin, a person becomes so distorted that the most natural path appears ugly to him, and the ugliest path appears right and beautiful. «مالنافى بناتك من حقّ...»

2- Possessing power, resources, and people is the initial desire of every prophet and reformer in carrying out the duty of guiding the ignorant. «لو انّ لى بكم قوة»

3- If there is no power, government, organization, or armed forces, even the prophets are paralyzed in preventing many evils. (114) «لو انّ لى بكم قوة»

4- If we can, we should prevent corruption; otherwise, we should distance ourselves from the corrupt environment. «لو ان لى بكم قوّة او آوى ...»

5- Sometimes the prophets were strangers even among their own people. «لو انّ لى بكم قوّة او آوى...»

6- Every measure is necessary to defend a guest and prevent insult to him. «آوى الى ركن شدید»

114) What Prophet Lot and the other Divine Prophets (علیهم السلام) wished for, Allah has bestowed upon us Iranians. Will we still not forbid evil?

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰلُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَن يَصِلُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَلَا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنكُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَكَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مُصِيبُهَا مَآ أَصَابَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ ٱلصُّبْحُ ۚ أَلَيْسَ ٱلصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ (۸۱)
They said, ‘O Lot, we are messengers of your Lord. They will never get at you. Set out with your family in a watch of the night; and none of you shall turn round, except your wife; indeed she will be struck by what strikes them. Indeed their tryst is the dawn. Is not the dawn [already] near?’ (81)

Key Points

The word "اَسرِ" (isri) is derived from the root "اَسراء" (isra), meaning movement during the night. The phrase "الاّ امراتكْ" (illa imra'atak) is either an exception from "أهلك" (ahlika), meaning: move all your family out during the night except your wife, or it is an exception from "لایَلتَفت" (la yaltafit), meaning: none of you should look back, except your wife, who, while leaving with you, will fall behind the caravan because she is concerned about the state of that corrupt people, and consequently, she will be afflicted by the Divine punishment.

Messages

1- Before destroying a people, Allah informs their Prophet. «انّا رُسل ربّك»

2- A believer must possess such speed of action and spiritual readiness that he can move quickly within a short period (one night). «فاسر... بقطع من اللیل»

3- To carry out a revolution and overturn a region of corruption, we should not pay attention to minor issues like our wealth and belongings. «و لایلتفت منكم احد»

4- Being related to the Prophet is not a means of salvation. «الاّامرأتك»

5- The system of values must be based on criteria (rules), not on relationships. «الاّ امرأتك»

6- Humans are free to choose their path; even the Prophet's wife, in his own house, chooses the path of deviation. «الاّ امرأتك» (A woman is an independent being.)

7- We must separate the account of religious figures from the account of their family and relatives. «الاّ امرأتك»

8- Allah's actions are based on timing. «موعدهم الصبح»

9- We should not rush Allah's wrath. «ألیس الصبح بقریب»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا جَعَلْنَا عَـٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا حِجَارَةً مِّن سِجِّيلٍ مَّنضُودٍ (۸۲)
So when Our edict came, We made its topmost part its nethermost, and We rained on it stones of laminar shale, (82)
مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا هِىَ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بِبَعِيدٍ (۸۳)
sent in from your Lord [for the profligate], never far from the wrongdoers. (83)

Key Points

Sijjīl (سِجّیل) means baked clay (or clods), something neither soft like mud nor hard like stone. And manḍūd (مَنضود) means piled up and continuous (one after the other). The meaning of the marked stone is either that each stone was designated for a specific person, or that these stones were different from earthly stones. This Ayah describes the end of the people of Lot.

In summary, it must be said that Prophet Lot عليه السلام was the Prophet of the people of Lot, and he lived during the time of Prophet Abraham عليه السلام and propagated his religion. He traveled to Palestine along with Prophet Abraham عليه السلام and then went towards a land called Mu'tafikāt, whose people were idolaters and openly practiced sodomy. Prophet Lot عليه السلام began fighting this great corruption and affliction, but no matter how much He tried, it had no effect, to the point where they threatened to expel him. They said: «لئن لم تنته یا لوط لتكوننّ من المخرجین» (115) O Lot! If you do not stop preaching, we will exile you. Finally, the angels assigned to the punishment descended, and after informing Prophet Abraham and then Prophet Lot علیهما السلام, they carried out their mission and turned the region of this corrupt people upside down. In Ayah 73 of Surah Al-Hijr, it is mentioned that a ṣayḥah (a mighty blast/cry) overtook the people of Lot. Perhaps due to this terrifying sound that turned the region upside down, stones were thrown from inside the earth towards the sky and then rained down upon them, similar to a volcanic eruption and the explosions that follow it. (116) Or perhaps, while the stones were raining down, a heavenly ṣayḥah also encompassed the region.

115) Shu‘arā: 167. 116) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The punishment for those who overturn human nature is that the city should be overturned upon them. «جعلنا عالیها سافلها»

2- Divine power can rain stones instead of water. «امطرنا ...حجارة»

3- Moral and sexual deviations constitute oppression (ẓulm). «من الظالمین»

4- Anyone who considers homosexuality permissible and free, like the Western world, must await the wrath of Allah and the overturning of the region of corruption. «و ما هى من الظالمین ببعید»

Ayah

۞ وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا تَنقُصُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ ۚ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُم بِخَيْرٍ وَإِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ مُّحِيطٍ (۸٤)
And to Midian [We sent] Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other god besides Him. Do not diminish the measure or the balance. Indeed I see that you are faring well, but I fear for you the punishment of an all-embracing day.’ (84)

Key Points

"Madyan" (whose current name is Ma'an and is referred to as Midian in the Torah) is a city east of the Gulf of Aqaba. Its people were descendants of Prophet Isma'il عليه السلام and traded with the countries of Egypt, Lebanon, and Palestine. Some also consider the word "Madyan" to be the name of a tribe whose people lived around Mount Sinai. The corruption of nations differs in every era and time, which must be fully noted. The corruption of the people of Lot was sexual deviation, and the corruption of the people of Shu'ayb was short-changing (in measure and weight) and economic corruption.

Messages

1- The sending of the Prophets عليهم السلام has been a Divine tradition and a continuous historical process. «والى مدین»

2- The mission of Prophet Shu'ayb was limited to the region of Madyan. «الى مدین»

3- Monotheism is based on the recognition of the One God. «اعبدوا اللَّه مالكم من اله غیره»

4- Worshipping Allah alone is not sufficient; dissociation from polytheists is also necessary. «مالكم من اله غیره»

5- The Prophets عليهم السلام did not only focus on doctrinal and moral issues, but also addressed economic matters and the factors leading to their corruption. «لا تنقصوا المكیال »

6- Prosperity and income alone are not deterrents against fraud and economic corruption. «لاتنقصوا المكیال... انى اراكم بخیر» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considered the meaning of «انّى اراكم بخیر» to be the abundance and low cost of goods.(117)

7- To enjoin good and forbid evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), one must give people dignity and encouragement. «انّى اراكم بخیر»

8- Drawing attention to the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) has been the priority of the call of all Prophets عليهم السلام. «اعبدوااللَّه... اخاف علیكم عذاب»

9- Belief in the Day of Reckoning is the basis for piety in all aspects of life. «لاتنقصوا المكیال، عذاب یوم محیط»

10- One cannot escape the wrath of Allah. «عذاب یوم محیط»

(117) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (۸٥)
‘O my people! Observe fully the measure and the balance, with justice, and do not cheat the people of their goods, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’ (85)

Key Points

The word "تَبخسوا" (tabkhasū) is derived from the root "بَخْس" (bakhs), meaning to diminish or reduce, and the phrase "لاتَعثوا" (lā ta‘thaw) means not to cause corruption.

Messages

1- Establishing justice and equity (qist) in all matters is among the achievements of the prophetic movement. "اوفوا... بالقسط"

2- If a sin becomes habitual in society, it must be rectified through repeated reminders and continuous follow-up. In this Ayah, the issue of observing weight and measure has been repeated several times. "اوفوا...لا تبخسوا"

3- Trade, transactions, and economic affairs must be subject to supervision and control. "اوفوا... لاتبخسوا"

4- Equity (qist) and justice must be observed towards all people, not just in favor of the believers. "لا تبخسوا الناس"

5- Diminishing the rights of people is not limited only to things that are weighed, measured, or counted by volume, but also includes all things that people trade and entrust to one another. "اشیاءهم"

6- Violating the rights of people in economic matters is one of the clearest examples of corruption (fasād). For an unhealthy economy leads society toward corruption. "لاتبخسوا", "مفسدین"

7- Worse and more severe than the act of corruption (fasād) itself is possessing the spirit and characteristic of corrupt individuals (mufsidīn). "لاتعثوا", "مفسدین"

Ayah

بَقِيَّتُ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ عَلَيْكُم بِحَفِيظٍ (۸٦)
‘What remains of Allah’s provision is better for you, should you be faithful, and I am not a keeper over you.’ (86)

Key Points

| "Baqiyyatullah" (The Remnant of Allah) in this Ayah means the income and profit that remains for a person from a lawful and God-pleasing capital, and it is one hundred percent lawful. However, in narrations (Ahadith), "Baqiyyatullah" is applied to every blessed being who remains for humanity by the will of Allah. This includes the believing soldiers who return victoriously from the battlefield, because they have remained by Divine will. The Imam of the Age (عجّل اللَّه تعالى فرجه الشریف) is also called "Baqiyyatullah," because that noble being has been preserved and kept remaining by the will of Allah for the guidance of the people. We read in narrations that one of the blessed names of that حضرت (His Holiness) is "Baqiyyatullah" (118), and we greet Him by this name: "Peace be upon you, O Baqiyyatullah upon His earth." When that حضرت makes His appearance in Mecca, He will recite this Ayah and say: I am that "Baqiyyatullah." Of course, the title "Baqiyyatullah" has also been given to the other Infallibles (علیهم السلام) (119).

118) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 46, p. 259. 119) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

| 1- A small but lawful and pure provision is better than abundant but unlawful income. «بقیّت اللّه خیر» (Baqiyyatullah is better). 2- This world is transient, and the Hereafter is eternal; therefore, focusing on the Hereafter is better than seeking the world. «بقیّت اللَّه خیر لكم» (Baqiyyatullah is better for you). 3- If we do not shortchange (in measure and weight), customers will increase, and consequently, our future profit will be greater and more pleasant. «بقیّت اللَّه خیر» (Baqiyyatullah is better). 4- Whoever considers the unlawful better than the lawful should doubt his faith. «ان كنتم مؤمنین» (If you are believers). 5- People are free in their beliefs, and even the Prophets do not impose anything upon them. «و ما انا علیكم بحفیظ» (And I am not a guardian over you). 6- If your wealth and life are ruined because of consuming the unlawful, I am not responsible. «و ما انا علیكم بحفیظ» (And I am not a guardian over you).

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۖ إِنَّكَ لَأَنتَ ٱلْحَلِيمُ ٱلرَّشِيدُ (۸۷)
They said, ‘O Shu‘ayb, does your worship require that we abandon what our fathers have been worshiping, or that we should not do with our wealth whatever we wish? You are indeed [a] gentle and sensible [person].’ (87)

Messages

1- All the Prophets (عليه السلام) faced opposition. «قالوا یا شعیب ...»

2- Prayer (Salat) existed in previous religions as well. «أصلوتك»

3- Prayer (Salat) was the most prominent manifestation of Shu'ayb's religion. «أصلوتك»

4- True prayer compels a person to guide others and enjoin them to good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) and forbid them from evil (Nahi 'anil Munkar). «أصلوتك تأمرك ان نترك»

5- Imitation of ancestors, when contradicting clear proof and text (Nass), is the source of many deviations. «یعبد آباؤنا»

6- Religion is separate neither from politics nor from economics. «أصلوتك...نترك ...فى اموالنا»

7- The path of the Prophets prevents unrestricted freedom. «نفعل فى اموالنا ما نشاء»

8- Ownership of something does not justify every type of consumption of it. «نترك... ان نفعل فى اموالنا ما نشاء» (In Shu'ayb's laws, there was a law controlling consumption.)

9- The glorification and praise of us by opponents is sometimes due to silence and failure to speak the truth. «انّك لانت الحلیم الرشید»

10- The praise and commendation of opponents sometimes carries an element of mockery (Istihza). «لانت الحلیم الرشید»

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَرَزَقَنِى مِنْهُ رِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُخَالِفَكُمْ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنْهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ ۚ إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا ٱلْإِصْلَـٰحَ مَا ٱسْتَطَعْتُ ۚ وَمَا تَوْفِيقِىٓ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ (۸۸)
He said, ‘O my people! Have you considered, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, who has provided me a good provision from Himself? I do not wish to oppose you by what I forbid you. I only desire to put things in order, as far as I can, and my success lies only with Allah: in Him I have put my trust, and to Him I turn penitently. (88)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the disbelievers objected, asking why they should not be free to dispose of their wealth as they wished. In this Ayah, Prophet Shu'ayb عليه السلام replies that if I restrict you from unconditional disposal of your wealth, it is for the sake of reforming your lives and society, not due to enmity, jealousy, narrow-mindedness, or other matters. This is because individual interests should not cause harm or damage to public interests.

Messages

1- Prophethood is based on miracles and clear evidence (Bayyinah). «كنت على بیّنة» (Laws are based on proof.)

2- The Prophets علیهم السلام attribute everything to Allah. «ربّى ، رزقنى»

3- We should not assume that a good life is achieved through short-changing (in measure/weight) and consuming unlawful earnings, because the Prophets and Divine Saints had good provision without being tainted by these sins. «رزقنى منه رزقاً حسنا» (Good provision is that which is not based on short-changing or oppression.)

4- Man's provision (Rizq) is from Allah and by His grace, not by cleverness. « رزقنى منه»

5- In response to the disbelievers who said: «اموالنا» (our wealth), Prophet Shu'ayb عليه السلام said: «رزقنى منه رزقاً حسنا» (He has provided me with good provision from Him), meaning, do not assume that I have financial problems.

6- The one who enjoins good and forbids evil must be a person of action before others. The Prophets were not merely planners and commanders; rather, they themselves were considered the best examples of those who acted upon their words. «و ما ارید ان اخالفكم الى ما انهاكم عنه»

7- The Prophets علیهم السلام not only did not act contrary to Divine commands, but they did not even contemplate transgression. «و ما ارید ان ...»

8- The goal of the Prophets علیهم السلام is the reformation of human beings and societies. «ان ارید الاّ الاصلاح»

9- The limit of the Prophets' work is their capacity and power, not hours, days, months, or years. «مااستطعت»

10- The intention for actions is ours, but the measure of success (Tawfiq) is from Allah. « ان ارید ، ما توفیقى الا بالله»

11- In our work, we must exert our utmost effort, but know that success is in the hands of Allah. «ما استطعت و ما توفیقى ...»

12- Reliance (Tawakkul) is fruitful only when accompanied by effort. «ما استطعت ، توكّلت»

13- The reformer must himself be righteous and possess faith, reliance (Tawakkul), repentance (Inabah), and fervor. «مااستطعت، علیه توكّلت و الیه اُنیب»

14- In this Ayah, the following recommendations are made for the one who enjoins good and forbids evil:

A: He must personally be a person of action. «ما ارید ان اُخالفكم الى ما انهاكم عنه»

B: His goal must be the reformation of society. «ما اُرید الاّ الاصلاح»

C: He must attribute the success (Tawfiq) of his work to Allah. «و ما توفیقى الاّ باللَّه»

D: He must always rely upon Him. «علیه توكّلت»

E: In difficulties, he must turn to Him for refuge. «الیه اُنیب»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ لَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شِقَاقِىٓ أَن يُصِيبَكُم مِّثْلُ مَآ أَصَابَ قَوْمَ نُوحٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ هُودٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ صَـٰلِحٍ ۚ وَمَا قَوْمُ لُوطٍ مِّنكُم بِبَعِيدٍ (۸۹)
O my people, do not let your defiance toward me lead you to be visited by the like of what was visited on the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih, and the people of Lot are not distant from you. (89)

Messages

1- Even when addressing opponents, we should speak with a good tone. «یا قوم»

2- Do not jeopardize your own destiny, happiness, and the welfare of society due to enmity toward one individual. «لا یجرمنّكم شقاقى»

3- The history of human nations is interconnected and similar, and recounting it serves as a lesson for reflection. «مثل ما اصاب»

4- We should not treat the bitter fate of previous generations as simple, trivial, seasonal, localized, individual, or accidental. « مثل ما اصاب قوم نوح...» (Prophets Noah, Hud, Salih, and Lot were before Prophet Shu'ayb).

5- Allah's Hand is open for wrath and punishment. He can destroy any nation in any manner, in any region, and at any time He wills. Just as He afflicted the people of Prophet Noah عليه السلام with drowning, the people of Prophet Hud with a storm, the people of Prophet Salih عليه السلام with a heavenly cry (Sayhah), and the people of Prophet Lot عليه السلام with destruction. «مثل ما اصاب قوم نوح او قوم هود او قوم صالح»

6- The best historical event for drawing lessons is the closest one. The people of Shu'ayb and the people of Lot were close both geographically and temporally. «و ما قوم لوط منكم ببعید»

Ayah

وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌ وَدُودٌ (۹٠)
Plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. My Lord is indeed all-merciful, all-affectionate.’ (90)

Key Points

The term wudd (وُدّ) refers to a friendship or love that possesses effects and continuity.

Messages

1- Alongside warning and cautioning opponents, the path to return and reform must also be presented to them. «استغفروا ...»

2- Seeking forgiveness and distancing oneself from sin is the prerequisite for returning to the path of truth. «استغفروا ربّكم ثمّ توبوا الیه»

3- If we seek forgiveness and repent, we will receive a positive response from our Lord. «استغفروا... انّ ربّى رحیمٌ ودود»

4- We should introduce Allah in such a way that the love for returning to Him becomes easy. «انّ ربّى رحیمٌ ودود»

5- Allah's mercy is not momentary; rather, it is continuous and possesses many effects and blessings. «انّ ربّى رحیم» (In addition to being a nominal sentence, Rahim is a superlative form [sighat al-mubalaghah]).

6- Allah is not only accepting of repentance but also loves the repentant person. «ودود»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًا ۖ وَلَوْلَا رَهْطُكَ لَرَجَمْنَـٰكَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْنَا بِعَزِيزٍ (۹۱)
They said, ‘O Shu‘ayb, we do not understand much of what you say. Indeed we see that you are weak amongst us, and were it not for your tribe, we would have stoned you, and you are not a formidable [challenge] for us.’ (91)

Key Points

The disbelievers used to say to Prophet Shu'ayb عليه السلام: We do not understand your words. What were the words of the Prophet that they did not understand? Was it not that He was saying: 1- I possess a miracle and clear proof. «كنت على بیّنة» 2- I do not have financial problems and I am not in need of you. «رزقنى منه رزقاً حسنا» 3- I act upon what I say. «ما اُرید ان اُخافكم...» 4- I seek only the reformation of society. «ان اُرید الاّ الاصلاح» 5- I strive to the best of my ability. «ما استطعتُ» 6- I rely upon Allah and turn to Him in repentance. «علیه توكّلت والیه اُنیب» 7- I warn you about the fate of those who came before. «مثل ما اصاب قوم نوح و...» However, the disbelievers both belittled His words, «ما نفقه» (We do not understand), and considered Him weak, «فینا ضعیفا» (weak among us), and deemed His family and relatives insignificant, «رهطك» (your clan), and threatened Him with severe torture, «لرجمناك» (we would certainly stone you), and disregarded His power and honor. «ما انت علینا بعزیز» (You are not mighty to us).

Messages

1- Prophets and divine preachers have endured the greatest insults in the path of inviting people to the straight path. «ما نفقه كثیراً ممّا تقول...»

2- Opponents of the Prophets lack reason and logic. Their logic is insult, torture, and killing. «لرجمناك و ما انت بعزیز»

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَهْطِىٓ أَعَزُّ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱتَّخَذْتُمُوهُ وَرَآءَكُمْ ظِهْرِيًّا ۖ إِنَّ رَبِّى بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ (۹۲)
He said, ‘O my people! Is my tribe more formidable in your sight than Allah, to whom you pay no regard? Indeed my Lord comprehends whatever you do. (92)

Messages

1- Observing the rights of the people, groups, parties, and international law should not supersede observing the Divine right. We must not allow the line of Allah to become isolated or overshadowed in political and social arenas. «أرهطى أعزّ»

2- Warning is necessary for obstinate individuals. «انّ ربّى بما تعملون محیط»

Ayah

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌ ۖ سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌ ۖ وَٱرْتَقِبُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُمْ رَقِيبٌ (۹۳)
O my people! Act according to your ability; I too am acting. Soon you will know who will be overtaken by a punishment that will disgrace him, and who is a liar. So be on the watch; I too will be watching along with you.’ (93)

Messages

1- Sometimes the prophets would despair of the effectiveness of their speech. «اعملوا على مكانتكم»

2- If you despair of guiding the people, remain steadfast upon your positions. «انّى عامل»

3- Where admonition is not effective, threat must be employed. «سوف تعلمون»

4- The response to a threat must be met with a threat. «رجمناك ، عذاب یخزیه»

5- The people considered Hadrat Shu'ayb a liar. «من هو كاذب»

6- We should fulfill our duty, and then await His aid. «انّى عامل، ارتقبوا»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا شُعَيْبًا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَأَخَذَتِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (۹٤)
When Our edict came, We delivered Shu‘ayb and the faithful who were with him by a mercy from Us. And the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers, whereat they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes, (94)

Messages

1- The wrath of Allah upon the oppressors may be delayed or hastened, but it is inevitable and devastating. «و لمّا جاء امرنا...»

2- Heavenly thunderbolts are not accidental; rather, they descend by Divine command. «امرنا»

3- Allah is the Savior and Supporter of the people of faith. «نجّینا»

4- Faith in the prophets must be accompanied by support for them. «آمنوا معه» instead of «آمنوا به»

5- Faith is the key to salvation. «نجّینا شعیباً و الذین آمنوا معه»

6- Disregard for the invitation of the prophets is oppression (zulm). «اَخذَتِ الذین ظلموا»

7- The ultimate fate of oppression and the oppressor is destruction. «اَخذَتِ الذین ظلموا»

8- The Divine punishment upon them was so swift and immense that they were unable to leave their homes. «دیارهم» (Apparently, the punishment descended at night.)

9- The Divine wrath arrives in such a way that it strips the oppressors of any power to flee. «جاثمین»

Ayah

كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ (۹٥)
as if they had never lived there. Behold, away with Midian!—just as Thamud was done away with! (95)

Key Points

Divine wrath is more destructive than any bomb, because during a bombing, more or less, some individuals find an opportunity to escape or are saved after the incident. But when the punishment of Allah and His wrath arrive, He eradicates them so completely that it is as if no one had ever inhabited that region.

Messages

1- There is no escape from Divine wrath. «كان لم یغنوا فیها»

2- Disassociation from oppressors and invoking death and curse upon them is a Divine and Quranic practice. «بعداً لمدین كما بعدت ثمود»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۹٦)
Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority (96)
إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۖ وَمَآ أَمْرُ فِرْعَوْنَ بِرَشِيدٍ (۹۷)
to Pharaoh and his elite, but they followed Pharaoh’s dictates, and Pharaoh’s dictates were not right-minded. (97)

Key Points

The number of verses in the Glorious Quran concerning Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) has not been matched for any other prophet or nation. This is because this Prophet has been mentioned more than 130 times and in over 30 Surahs, the most concise mention of which is in this Surah. The word "سُلطان" (Sultan) means dominance or authority (Sultah). Sometimes it refers to apparent (physical) dominance, and sometimes it is used for logical and argumentative dominance.

Messages

1- The claim of representation on behalf of Allah must be accompanied by a sign and proof. «أرسلنا موسى بایاتنا وسلطان مبین»

2- The logic and miracles of the Prophets (علیهم السلام) are clear and understandable for all people. «مبین»

3- Among the most important philosophies of the Prophets' (علیهم السلام) missions is the eradication of tyranny (Taghut-zadā'ī), and the freedom and liberation of human beings from the bondage of others. «الى فرعون»

4- Many people have been followers of tyrants (Taghuts). «فاتّبعوا امر فرعون»

5- The propaganda slogans of the tyrants must be neutralized. Because Pharaoh falsely claimed, "I only guide you to the path of rectitude (Roshd)"; «و ما اَهدیكم الاّ سبیل الرشاد» (120), this verse replies: «و ما امر فرعون برشید» (And the command of Pharaoh was not right/wise).

6- Knowledge and recognition alone are not always the factor preventing deviation. (Moses clarified the truth for the people with proof and evidence, but Pharaoh invited people to deviation, and the people followed Pharaoh.) (120) Ghafir, 29.

Ayah

يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فَأَوْرَدَهُمُ ٱلنَّارَ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْوِرْدُ ٱلْمَوْرُودُ (۹۸)
On the Day of Resurrection he will lead his people and conduct them into the Fire: an evil goal for the incoming! (98)

Key Points

The word "وِرْد" (wird) means the water that a person enters. The word "ورود" (wurūd) originally means movement towards water and approaching it, but gradually it has been applied to any type of entering into something.

Messages

1- The sign of the soundness of programs and laws is that they lead to entering Paradise, not Hellfire. «وما امر فرعون برشید یقدم قومه...»

2- Those who are leaders and Imams of the people in this world will also be pioneers toward Paradise or Hell in the Hereafter. The Imam of Light (the just one) will guide people toward Light (Paradise), and the Imam of Fire (the oppressor) will guide them toward Fire (Hell). «یقدم قومه یوم القیامة»

Ayah

وَأُتْبِعُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِۦ لَعْنَةً وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ بِئْسَ ٱلرِّفْدُ ٱلْمَرْفُودُ (۹۹)
They are pursued by a curse in this [world], as well as on the Day of Resurrection; evil is the award conferred [upon them]! (99)

Messages

1- Whoever follows the Pharaohs and tyrants will be pursued by infamy and curse in this world and the Hereafter. «اتّبعوا امر فرعون، و اُتبعوا ... لعنة»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّهُۥ عَلَيْكَ ۖ مِنْهَا قَآئِمٌ وَحَصِيدٌ (۱٠٠)
These are from the accounts of the townships, which We recount to you. Of them, there are some that still stand, and some that have been mown down. (100)

Key Points

It is possible that the intent of the Ayah is that in some cases, We destroyed only the people themselves, which is why their ruins remain, but in other cases, We annihilated them along with all their ruins and homes. The narration of stories in the Quran has important roles and effects, because: 1- It is appealing and attractive. 2- It paves the way for greater penetration and influence of rational and demonstrative proofs. 3- It is another proof of the miracle of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, showing how an unlettered person knows such news. 4- It is a lesson for the listeners.

Messages

1- Historical narration is necessary to the extent that it leads to growth, admonition, and warning. «من انباء» (Some of the history of past peoples, not all of it.)

2- The history of Noah, Hud, Salih, Lot, Shu'ayb, and Moses is an important part of human history. «انباء» («انباء» means important news.)

3- The Quran is a source for becoming acquainted with past history and the fate of previous generations.

4- Narrating correct, documented, and instructive stories is a Divine task; let us not neglect it. «نقصّه»

5- Quranic stories are the most truthful and correct narratives, because their Speaker is Allah. «نقصّه»

6- The account of the history of nations is separate from the account of their ruins and remnants, because history is an enduring matter, even if there may be no trace of their ruins and remnants. «حَصید»

Ayah

وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ فَمَآ أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ءَالِهَتُهُمُ ٱلَّتِى يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ لَّمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا زَادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍ (۱٠۱)
We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. When your Lord’s edict came, their gods whom they would invoke besides Allah were of no avail to them in any wise, and they did not increase them in anything but ruin. (101)

Key Points

The word "تَتبِیب" (tatbīb) comes from "تَب" (tabb), meaning persistence in loss, destruction, and annihilation.

Messages

  1. Punishing and chastising the oppressor is not oppression; rather, it is the essence of justice. "وما ظلمناهم"
  2. The destiny of man depends on his own deeds and actions. "ظلموا انفسهم"
  3. Other than Allah, no one and nothing else can save man. "فما اغنت"
  4. Against the Divine Will, no one and nothing has the power to resist. "من شى"

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَخْذُ رَبِّكَ إِذَآ أَخَذَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَهِىَ ظَـٰلِمَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ أَخْذَهُۥٓ أَلِيمٌ شَدِيدٌ (۱٠۲)
Such is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the townships that are wrongdoing. Indeed His seizing is painful and severe. (102)

Messages

1- Allah’s wrath and His grace follow a stable tradition and an eternal law, not a sudden or accidental matter. «كذلك»

2- Oppressive nations are exposed to Divine wrath. «كذلك أخذ ربّك...»

3- Warning, when used appropriately, is one of the effective educational factors. «اخذ ربك»

4- Allah’s wrath is among the affairs of His Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «اخذ ربك»

5- The cause of misfortune and the descent of Divine wrath is the people themselves. «اخذ القرى و هى ظالمة»

6- Allah’s wrath descends when oppression covers the face of the region. «اخذ القرى وهى ظالمة»

7- We must not consider Allah’s wrath simple or insignificant. «ان اخذه الیم شدید»

Ayah

إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّمَنْ خَافَ عَذَابَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَّجْمُوعٌ لَّهُ ٱلنَّاسُ وَذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَّشْهُودٌ (۱٠۳)
There is indeed a sign in that for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a day on which all mankind will be gathered, and it is a day witnessed [by all creatures]. (103)

Messages

1- The narration of a story must have a direction and a purpose. «لَایةً»

2- Only those who believe in the Hereafter take the true stories of the Quran as a lesson for themselves. «لایة لمن خاف عذاب الآخره»

3- The fear of the Day of Judgment (even if it is not based on certainty, but rather on the probability of danger) causes one to take heed. «لایة لمن خاف عذاب الآخره»

4- The gathering and presence of people on the Day of Judgment is a compulsory matter, not a voluntary one. «مجموع له الناس» rather than «یوم یجمع الناس»

Ayahs

وَمَا نُؤَخِّرُهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍ مَّعْدُودٍ (۱٠٤)
And We do not defer it but for a determinate term. (104)
يَوْمَ يَأْتِ لَا تَكَلَّمُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ فَمِنْهُمْ شَقِىٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ (۱٠٥)
The day it comes, no one shall speak except by His leave. [On that day,] some of them will be wretched and [some] felicitous. (105)

Key Points

The word "sa'adah" (سعادت) means the availability of the means of blessing, and the word "shaqawah" (شقاوت) means the availability of the means of affliction.

Messages

  1. The time for the establishment of the Resurrection and the end of the world has been specified and determined beforehand. «اجل معدود»

  2. The interval set for the establishment of the Resurrection is also for the sake of pursuing a goal. «لاجل » and He did not say: «الى اجل».

  3. On the Day of Resurrection, silence prevails over everyone, except those individuals who have permission to speak. «لاتكلّم نفس الا باذنه»

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ شَقُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلنَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌ وَشَهِيقٌ (۱٠٦)
As for the wretched, they shall be in the Fire: their lot therein will be groaning and wailing. (106)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ فَعَّالٌ لِّمَا يُرِيدُ (۱٠۷)
They shall remain in it for as long as the heavens and the earth endure—except what your Lord may wish; indeed your Lord does whatever He desires. (107)

Key Points

Zafīr (زَفیر) is a cry accompanied by the expulsion of breath, and Shahīq (شَهیق) is a prolonged moan coupled with the inhalation of breath. Some have also considered Zafīr to be the beginning of a donkey's bray and Shahīq to be its end. Not all individuals who enter Hell are equal in the duration of their stay there. Some are released from Hell after a short period, others remain in Hell for a very long time and are eventually freed, but a group will remain there forever and will never leave it. The word khulūd (خُلود) in cases where it is accompanied by the word abad (اَبَد), such as: «خالدین فیها ابدا» (121), means permanence and eternity, not merely a long duration. Although in this verse khulūd (eternal stay) is stated to last as long as the heavens and the earth endure, and the Quran has specified the duration of their establishment (122), it still signifies eternity and permanence. This is because «مادامت السموات» (as long as the heavens endure) is an idiom in the Arabic language and literature used to denote eternity and perpetuity, even though the heavens are not eternal.

121) Nisā', 169 and Jinn, 23. 122) Ibrāhīm, 48 and Anbiyā', 104.

Messages

1- Man causes his own wretchedness through his choices. (Shaqū) شَقُوا, not (Shuqu) شُقُوا.

2- Allah's hand is open to save a group of the inhabitants of Hell (due to lesser crimes, deserving of pardon, or any other reason). «الاّ ما شاء ربّك » (Except what your Lord wills).

Ayah

۞ وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۖ عَطَآءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ (۱٠۸)
As for the felicitous, they will be in paradise. They will remain in it for as long as the heavens and the earth endure—except what your Lord may wish—an endless bounty. (108)

Messages

  1. The cause of human wretchedness and misery is their own choice and will, but their happiness is through Divine success. Therefore, for wretchedness, the phrase «شَقُوا» is used, and for happiness, the phrase «سُعِدوا» is used.

  2. Although the Divine Hand is open in every matter, even for expelling the people of Paradise from Paradise, «الاّ ما شاء ربك» (except what your Lord wills), yet, considering the last part of this Ayah, «عطاء غیر مجذوذ» (an uninterrupted gift), and the promises given in other verses, He will not do this, and when someone enters Paradise, he will remain there forever.

Ayah

فَلَا تَكُ فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّمَّا يَعْبُدُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ مَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُهُم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ وَإِنَّا لَمُوَفُّوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ غَيْرَ مَنقُوصٍ (۱٠۹)
So do not be in doubt about what these worship: they worship just as their fathers worshiped before, and We shall surely pay them their full share, undiminished. (109)

Messages

1- A leader must be so firm and decisive in his path and goal that the deviation of the community cannot cause him to waver or become weak. «فلاتك»

2- Addressing and admonishing prominent figures has a greater psychological impact on the people. «فلاتك»

3- Idolaters and polytheists lack logic and reasoning. «یعبد آباؤهم»

4- Ancestors play a role in the deeds and behavior of future generations. «یعبد آباؤهم»

5- Following traditions and paying attention to the thoughts and beliefs of ancestors is not valuable everywhere. «یعبد آباؤهم»

6- Allah does not commit injustice, even towards disbelievers and polytheists. «لمُوَفوهم نصیبهم»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ فَٱخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ (۱۱٠)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book, but differences arose about it, and were it not for a prior decree of your Lord, a decision would have been made between them; indeed they are in grave doubt concerning it. (110)

Messages

1- In no period of history have all individuals of a nation been unanimous in belief. «فاختلف فیه»

2- We should not be distressed or worried by the disagreement of people regarding religion, the belief of some, and the disbelief (kufr) of others, as this is not a new phenomenon. «اتینا موسى الكتاب فاختلف فیه»

3- Although in some verses of the Glorious Qur'an, the Torah is called the Book of Light, (123) people still disagreed concerning that Light. «فاختلف فیه »

4- Allah Himself observes His own traditions (Sunan) and does not violate them, because before anyone else, it is the Lawgiver who must respect the law. «و لولا كلمةسبقت من ربّك» (Allah has not made this world the place for final judgment.)

5- The Divine traditions are based on His station of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «سبقت من ربّك»

6- Granting respite to those who disagree regarding the Heavenly Book is among the Divine traditions. «كلمة سبقت من ربّك»

Ayah

وَإِنَّ كُلًّا لَّمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۱۱۱)
Your Lord will indeed recompense everyone fully for their works. Indeed, He is well aware of what they do. (111)

Messages

1- In the divine worldview, no action is without response and reward. «لیوفّینّهم»

2- There is no deficiency or shortage in the divine reward or punishment. «لیوفّینّهم»

3- The complete recompense from Allah is based on His complete awareness of people's actions. «خبیر»

Ayah

فَٱسْتَقِمْ كَمَآ أُمِرْتَ وَمَن تَابَ مَعَكَ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۱۱۲)
So be steadfast, just as you have been commanded—[you] and whoever has turned [to Allah] with you—and do not overstep the bounds. Indeed, He watches what you do. (112)

Key Points

It is narrated in traditions that the Prophet said: Surah Hud made me old (or aged me). And it has been said that the meaning intended by His Holiness was this Ayah from Surah Hud. (124) Although the first phrase of this Ayah, «فاستقم كما امرت» (So be steadfast as you have been commanded), also appears in another Surah (125) of the Glorious Qur'an, this specific Ayah has become famous for this characteristic. Perhaps the weight (or gravity) of this Ayah lies in the existence of other historical aspects not mentioned explicitly in the Ayah, namely, the steadfastness of the companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, not all of whom remained loyal, and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was distressed by their disloyalty and instability.

(124) Tafsir Namuneh. (125) Shura, 15

Messages

1- Now that Allah gives everyone their punishment and reward without any reduction or deficiency, then be steadfast. «كلا لیوفّینّهم...فاستقم»

2- The result (or lesson) of the history of the Prophets is steadfastness. «فاستقم»

3- In steadfastness and perseverance, the leader must be the pioneer for everyone. «فاستقم» (The conditions in Mecca for the early Muslims were difficult and required steadfastness.)

4- Perseverance is valuable when it is present in all matters: in worship, in guidance, in enduring hardships, and the like. «فاستقم»

5- Action must be according to the explicit text and Divine command, not based on analogy (Qiyas), preference (Istihsan), imagination, or similar things. «كما اُمرت»

6- The perseverance of the leader is fruitless without the accompaniment and perseverance of the community (Ummah). «و مَن تاب معك»

7- Turning and returning to Allah demands a heavy price, which is steadfastness and perseverance on the straight path. «و مَن تاب معك»

8- Everything must be according to the command, not excessiveness or transgression (Tughyan). «كما اُمرت... و لاتطغوا»

9- The leader and the community must move on the straight path and avoid extremism (Ifraat) and negligence (Tafreet). «فاستقم ، و لاتطغوا»

10- Steadfastness in religion brings Divine reward. «فاستقم... انه بما تعملون بصیر»

Ayah

وَلَا تَرْكَنُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ فَتَمَسَّكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ (۱۱۳)
Do not incline toward the wrongdoers, lest the Fire should touch you, and you will not have any friend besides Allah; then you will not be helped. (113)

Key Points

We read in narrations that affection and love for the oppressor and obedience to him are considered instances of inclining towards the oppressor (rukūn ilā al-ẓālim). In one narration, it states: Do not place hope in the tyrant, even if he is your relative and friend. «و ان كان حمیما قریباً»(126) In Al-Kafi, this narration also appears: If you are content with the oppressor being alive for the duration it takes him to put his hand in his pocket to give a gift, you have inclined and relied upon the oppressor, which Allah has forbidden.(127) Since obedience to the Ulu al-Amr (those in authority) is obligatory, and on the other hand, inclining towards the oppressor is forbidden (haram), the Ulu al-Amr cannot be an oppressor. Rather, they must also be infallible (ma'sum), because sin is considered an instance of oppression (ẓulm). Inclining and relying upon the oppressor leads to the Fire and the punishment of Allah, so what will be the condition of the oppressor himself?!

126) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 127) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 108.

Messages

1- Any inclination or reliance upon domestic or foreign tyrants is forbidden. «لاتركنوا» (Tyrants are not worthy of being followed or led.)

2- Relying upon tyrants is a major sin (gunah-e kabirah). (Any sin for which the Quran promises the Fire is a major sin.) «لا تركنوا... فتمسّكم النار»

3- Instead of clinging to the oppressor, we should rely upon Allah. «و مالكم من دون اللّه من اولیاء»

4- The result of relying upon tyrants is alienation and loneliness. «ثم لاتنصرون»

Ayahs

وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ طَرَفَىِ ٱلنَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْحَسَنَـٰتِ يُذْهِبْنَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ذِكْرَىٰ لِلذَّٰكِرِينَ (۱۱٤)
Maintain the prayer at the two ends of the day, and during the early hours of the night. Indeed good deeds efface misdeeds. That is an admonition for the mindful. (114)
وَٱصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۱٥)
And be patient; indeed Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous. (115)

Key Points

In narrations, the meaning of the two ends of the day (طرفى النهار) is stated as morning (dawn) and sunset (Maghrib), and the meaning of the early parts of the night (زلفاً من اللیل) is the 'Isha prayer. (128) Of course, this Ayah is not intended to enumerate all daily prayers. A person was distressed by his evil deeds, so after the prayer in the mosque, he asked the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to administer the prescribed punishment (Hadd) upon him. The Prophet told him: Since you participated in the congregational prayer after that transgression, Allah has forgiven you. (129) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about a person who acquired earnings through illegitimate means (Taghut) and, hoping in the Ayah «انّ الحسنات یذهبن السیئات» (Indeed, good deeds remove evil deeds), helps the poor and performs Hajj. The Imam said: «انّ الخطیئة لاتكفر الخطیئة» (A sin cannot expiate a sin). (130) What he has acquired belongs to the people, and disposing of people's property in any form is forbidden (Haram).

The Most Hope-Giving Ayah of the Quran Imam Ali عليه السلام entered upon a group and asked them: Do you know which Ayah in the Quran gives the most hope?! Each person cited an Ayah according to his understanding: * Some said: The Ayah «انّ اللَّه لایغفر اَن یشرك به و یغفر مادون ذلك» (131) meaning that Allah forgives sins other than polytheism (Shirk). * Others believed it was this Ayah: «و مَن یعمل سوء او یظلم نفسه ثمّ یستغفر اللّه یجد اللّه غفوراً رحیما» (132) meaning that whoever commits evil or wrongs himself, but then seeks Allah's forgiveness, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful. * A number stated the Ayah: «یا عبادى الذین اسرفوا على انفسهم لاتقنطوا من رحمة اللّه انّ اللّه یغفر الذنوب جمیعاً» (133) meaning: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves! Do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, He forgives all sins. * A few also held the view that it was this Ayah: «والذین اذا فعلوا فاحشة او ظلموا انفسهم ذكروا اللّه فاستغفروا لذنوبهم و مَن یغفر الذنوب الاّ اللّه» (134) meaning those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins—and who forgives sins except Allah?

After the Imam heard their opinions, He said: I heard from my beloved, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, who said: The most hope-giving Ayah in the Quran is this Ayah: «اقم الصلاة طرفى النهار و زلفاً من اللیل انّ الحسنات یُذهبن السیئات» (Establish the prayer at the two ends of the day and in the early hours of the night. Indeed, good deeds remove evil deeds.) Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله continued: O Ali! By the God Who sent me as a bearer of good news and a warner to the people, when a person performs ablution (Wudu) for prayer, his sins are shed, and when he faces the Qiblah, he becomes purified. O Ali! The example of the one who establishes the daily prayers is like a person who washes himself five times every day in a river that flows in front of his house. (135)

128) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq. 129) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 130) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq. 131) An-Nisa, 48. 132) An-Nisa, 110. 133) Az-Zumar, 53. 134) Aal-i-Imran, 134. 135) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Kanz al-Daqaiq.

Messages

1- The leader must be a role model for the people. «فاستقم... اقم...»

2- Prayer must be performed with complete conditions and etiquette. The word Iqamah (اقامه) implies the meaning of perfection and completeness. «اقم الصلاة»

3- Prayer is the strength of the believers' hearts. Since the previous verses commanded steadfastness and non-reliance on oppressors, and these matters require high morale, Allah, therefore, commands the strengthening of the spirit through the leverage of prayer in this Ayah. «فاستقم... و اقم الصلاة»

4- Prayers must be performed at their appointed times. «طرفى النهار»

5- Prayer is the clearest example of a good deed. «اقم... ان الحسنات...»

6- Human actions influence one another. «ان الحسنات یذهبن السیئات»

7- The command to pray is the clearest admonition from Allah. «ذلك ذكرى» (Prayer is the remembrance of Allah.)

8- Not everyone benefits from divine reminders. «ذكرى للذاكرین»

9- Establishing and maintaining prayer requires perseverance and patience. (136) «اقم الصلاة ، واصبر» (136) In another Ayah, He says: «وأمر اهلك بالصلوة و اصطبر علیها» (And enjoin prayer upon your family and be steadfast in its observance.) (Taha, 132).

Ayah

فَلَوْلَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ بَقِيَّةٍ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْفَسَادِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّنْ أَنجَيْنَا مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَٱتَّبَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَآ أُتْرِفُوا۟ فِيهِ وَكَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ (۱۱٦)
Why were there not among the generations before you a remnant [of the wise] who might forbid corruption in the earth, except a few of those whom We delivered from among them? Those who were wrongdoers pursued that in which they had been granted affluence, and they were a guilty lot. (116)

Key Points

The word Baqiyyah (بَقیّة), which appears 3 times in the Quran, means 'remainder' or 'what is left over,' but usually in the Arabic language, it refers to grace and power. Perhaps this is because man stores and preserves valuable goods, or perhaps because those individuals who remain on the scene are those who possess greater power and strength. In this Ayah, Allah criticizes a group for not equipping themselves with knowledge and power in order to warn society. This warning, using the tone of lawlā (لولا - why not/if only), has been repeated many times in the Quran, including: the Ayah, «فلولا نفر من كل فرقة منهم طائفة»(137) and the Ayah, «لولا ینهاهم الربّانیون و الاحبار»(138).

Since Ayah 114 concerns Salat (prayer) and this Ayah concerns Nahy an al-Munkar (forbidding evil), it is appropriate to point to verses in the Holy Quran where the issues of Salat and Amr bi al-Ma'ruf wa Nahy an al-Munkar (enjoining good and forbidding evil) are mentioned side-by-side and influence one another. Among these verses are:

A: «انّ الصلوة تنهى عن الفحشاء و المنكر»(139) Certainly, prayer restrains man from indecencies and evils. B: «انّ الحسنات یذهبن السیئات»(140) Indeed, good deeds (like prayer) wipe away bad deeds. C: «اقم الصلوة و أمر بالمعروف و انه عن المنكر»(141) Establish prayer, enjoin good, and forbid evil. D: «الذین ان مكنّاهم فى الارض اقاموا الصلوة و اتوا الزكاة و امروا بالمعروف و نَهَوا عن المنكر»(142) The true believers are those who, if We establish them in the land, establish prayer, pay Zakat, enjoin good, and forbid evil.

137) Tawbah, 122. 138) Ma'idah, 63. 139) Ankabut, 45. 140) Hud, 114. 141) Luqman, 14. 142) Hajj, 41.

Messages

1- In all eras, individuals who are indifferent toward social corruption are condemned and criticized. «فلو لا من القرون...»

2- The greatest responsibility in forbidding evil rests upon those possessing knowledge and power. «اولوا بقیّة»

3- In every society, reformers and compassionate individuals are in the minority. «الاّ قلیلا»

4- The secret to the downfall of previous nations was indifference and the abandonment of forbidding evil. «الا قلیلاً ممّن انجینا»

5- The result of the compassion of reformers and the reform of society is salvation from Divine wrath. «انجینا»

6- Oppression, extravagance, and crime are the sides of the ominous triangle of downfall and destruction. «ظلموا، اترفوا، مجرمین»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُهَا مُصْلِحُونَ (۱۱۷)
Your Lord would never destroy the townships unjustly while their inhabitants were bringing about reform. (117)

Key Points

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The meaning of Iṣlāḥ (rectification/reform) in the Ayah is the fairness of people towards one another. (143) The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: O Ibn Mas'ud! Deal with people justly, be their well-wisher, and show them mercy. If you are such, and Allah becomes angry with the people of a town where you reside, He will look towards you and, because of you, show them mercy. (144)

143) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 144) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 109.

Messages

1- The wrath of Allah has a reason. «يُهْلِكُ الْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ»

2- Being merely Ṣāliḥ (righteous) is not enough; one must be Muṣliḥ (a reformer). «مَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ... وَأَهْلُهَا مُصْلِحُونَ» (One of the factors for safety from divine punishment is the people's pursuit of reform.)

Ayah

وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ لَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً ۖ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ (۱۱۸)
Had your Lord wished, He would have made mankind one community; but they continue to differ, (118)

Key Points

In the Glorious Qur'an, the issue of human freedom in choosing their path and belief has been mentioned repeatedly. Meaning, although Allah guides people towards Himself, He never compels them in this path, nor has He given the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله the right to force people. Rather, He has only asked the Prophet to remind the people and recall the path to them. «انّما انت مذكّر، لست علیهم بمصیطر»(145). Therefore, Allah inspires the path of piety (Taqwa) and wickedness (Fujur) in such a way that the human nature (Fitrah) perceives good and evil from within, and from the outside, He sends the Prophets علیهم السلام for guidance, and it is man himself who chooses his path with complete will and free choice.

145) Ghashiyah, 21.

Messages

1- The Divine tradition in the creation of man is based on freedom and the choice of beliefs and ideas. «لو شاء ربّك لجعل الناس امة واحدة»

2- The Will of Allah is undeniable (cannot be violated). «لوشاء ربّك لجعل»

3- At no time have all people been uniform (of one accord). «ولایزالون مختلفین»

4- The reason for people's differences is their power of freedom. «لو شاء... ولایزالون مختلفین»

Ayah

إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ رَبُّكَ ۚ وَلِذَٰلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ۗ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۱۹)
except those on whom your Lord has mercy—and that is why He created them—and the word of your Lord has been fulfilled: ‘I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together!’ (119)

Key Points

Some have said: the meaning of the Ayah is that people are constantly in disagreement, except for those who, under the shadow of divine grace and mercy, are not afflicted by disagreement, and Allah created them for the sake of this unity of word (or purpose). (146) The philosophy of human creation has been presented in the verses of the Quran under various titles: In one place, we read: «و ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس الاّ لیعبدون» (147), where the reason for the creation of jinn and humans is considered to be their worship. In another place, He says: «الذى خلق الموت و الحیاة لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملا» (148), meaning that Allah created death and life to test which of you is best in deed. And in the above Ayah, it is also stated: «لذلك خلقهم», meaning We created humans so that they may be covered by His mercy. Apparently, it seems that the philosophy of human creation has three different titles: worship, testing, and mercy. However, upon closer examination, we realize that all three subjects share a common point, which is the spiritual and moral perfection of human beings. In the previous Ayah, it was mentioned that man is free, and at the end of this Ayah, He says: We shall surely fill Hell with jinn and humans. What is understood by combining these two verses is that man is free to choose his path, but he becomes deserving of Hell due to his acceptance of the false path. It is mentioned in numerous narrations that the clear example of people's disagreement is the issue of the infallible leadership, where naturally, except for the group that is covered by divine mercy and follows the path of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام and the infallible Imams, the rest of the people have deviated. (149) Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said: Allah created man so that he may perform deeds and become deserving of mercy. (150)

146) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 147) Adh-Dhariyat, 56. 148) Al-Mulk, 2. 149) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqa'iq. 150) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn; Tawhid Saduq, p. 403.

Messages

1- The only way to eliminate disagreements is through divine grace and mercy. «الاّ مَن رحم ربّك» 2- The factor of unity is solely divine assistance, while worldly and material centers are fleeting, temporary, one-dimensional, and illusory matters. «الاّ مَن رحم ربّك» 3- The philosophy of human creation is the acceptance and reception of divine mercy. «و لذلك خلقهم» 4- Allah has made certain laws and traditions incumbent upon Himself. «و تمّت كلمة ربك» 5- Allah's inevitable wrath is mentioned after His grace, the sending of Prophets and Books, admonition, warning, and granting respite. «رحم ربك... لاملأنّ جهنّم» 6- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon people by placing innate nature (Fitrah) and intellect within man and sending Prophets and heavenly Books from without. «و تمّت كلمة ربّك» 7- Hell has a limit (or capacity). «لاملأنّ جهنّم» 8- Jinn, like humans, are subject to religious obligation (Taklif), punishment, and reward. «من الجنّة والناس»

Ayah

وَكُلًّا نَّقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلرُّسُلِ مَا نُثَبِّتُ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۚ وَجَآءَكَ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَقُّ وَمَوْعِظَةٌ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۲٠)
Whatever We relate to you of the accounts of the apostles are those by which We strengthen your heart, and there has come to you in this [surah] the truth and an advice and admonition for the faithful. (120)

Messages

1- Narrating important parts of history and providing awareness of them is one of the methods of education. «نقُص علیك من انباء الرسل»

2- Relating the biographies of the Prophets علیهم السلام carries educational weight, and no word or point within it is without reason. «كلّاً»

3- The Quranic stories are selections from the biographies of the Prophets علیهم السلام. «نقصّ علیك من انباء الرّسل»

4- The Quranic stories are purposeful, not a means of entertainment or distraction. «نقصّ علیك... ما نثبّت»

5- When narrating a story, attention must be paid to those segments that carry valuable weight. «ما نثبّت»

6- The best story is that which brings tranquility to the heart. «ما نثبّت به فؤادك»

7- Allah is the One who brings peace to hearts. «نثبّت به فؤادك»

8- The Prophets علیهم السلام also need spiritual reinforcement. «نثبّت به فؤادك»

9- True and lasting tranquility is never achieved through falsehood. «نثبّت به فؤادك و جائك فى هذه الحق»

10- A preacher is successful in his propagation and admonition only when he possesses complete stability and assurance. «نثبت به فؤادك»

11- All Quranic stories are true and are accompanied by a type of acceptable proof and evidence. «الحقّ»

12- In propagation, there must first be tranquility of heart, then reasoned discourse, and finally admonition. «فؤادك، حق، موعظه»

13- The best methods of education are effective only for receptive individuals. «ذكرى للمؤمنین»

Ayahs

وَقُل لِّلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنَّا عَـٰمِلُونَ (۱۲۱)
Say to those who do not have faith, ‘Act according to your ability; we too are acting. (121)
وَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مُنتَظِرُونَ (۱۲۲)
And wait! We too are waiting.’ (122)

Messages

1- Conveying warnings and glad tidings is the duty of the prophets. «قل»

2- Man is free, and the prophets علیهم السلام do not impose anything upon him. «اعملوا على مكانتكم»

3- Whoever does not accept admonition and for whom reminders are not beneficial must be warned (or threatened). «اعملوا على مكانتكم»

4- The propagator must have complete faith in his path and goal. «انّا عاملون , انّا منتظرون»

5- In propagation, one must speak to every person according to the measure of his own intellect and understanding; with stubborn and obstinate individuals, [one must speak] with severity and warning. «انتظروا»

6- A good outcome awaits the Prophet and the believers. «انّا منتظرون»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُ ٱلْأَمْرُ كُلُّهُۥ فَٱعْبُدْهُ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲۳)
To Allah belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth, and to Him all matters are returned. So worship Him and trust in Him. Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do. (123)

Key Points

The knowledge of the Unseen belongs absolutely to Allah, but by divine permission and will, the Prophets also possess the knowledge of the Unseen. «عالم الغیب فلایظهر على غیبه احداً الاّ مَن ارتضى من رسول»(151)

151) Al-Jinn, 26.

Messages

1- Awareness of the secrets of the universe belongs exclusively to Allah. «للّه غیب السموات و الارض»

2- The earth and the heavens (the entire existence), besides their apparent aspect, also possess an unseen and hidden aspect. «للّه غیب السموات و الارض»

3- The reference point for everything is Him, and the return of everything is towards Him. «الیه یرجع الامر كله»

4- Belief in the Unseen is the foundation for worship and reliance upon Allah. «للّه غیب... فاعبده»

5- Whoever relies upon Allah will be placed under His favor. «و توكّل علیه و ما ربّك بغافل...»

6- Awareness of human actions is among the attributes of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «و ما ربّك...»
